Japanese: 同日　某時刻 レストラン　吐麗美庵
English: January 6 Trés Bien

Japanese: ひゃあ‥‥ なんか、すごいね。
English: Wow! Look at this place.

Japanese: なんなんだ‥‥この、ムセかえる ほどにキツい、花のニオイは‥‥
English: "Look"? More like "smell"... What is with the suffocating scent of flowers in here...?

Japanese: ‥‥でも、こういうフンイキ、 女の子はスキなのかな。
English: Then again, girls like that kind of thing, right?

Japanese: いやあ、あたしはちょっと、 こういうのは‥‥
English: Actually, I'm not all that into it...

Japanese: ダレも出てこないね。 おルスかな。
English: No one's coming to seat us. Maybe there's no one here.

Japanese: バカな。レストランだぞ。 それも、開店中の。
English: Don't be silly, Maya. This is a restaurant, and it's open for business.

Japanese: ごめんくださーい。
English: HELLO! ANYONE HERE?

Japanese: し、信じられないけど、 ルスみたいだな。
English: I don't believe it! There really isn't anyone here!

Japanese: とりあえず、手がかりを探そうよ！
English: Perfect! Let's get intrusive!

Japanese: 今なら、なんでも 持ち出しほうだいだよ！
English: If there's no one here, we can take anything we want!

Japanese: （それもそうだな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I suppose we can...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 レストラン　吐麗美庵
English: January 6 Trés Bien

Japanese: あらあ！　ボンジュウル。 ‥‥いらっしゃいませえ。
English: Oh là là! Bon-JOUR! Welcome to la Trés Bien!

Japanese: あ。どうも、こんにち‥‥は‥‥
English: Oh! Hel... lo...

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃん、何を こおりついてるんだ‥‥？　う）
English: (What happened to Maya? She's frozen stiff...)

Japanese: ビアンヴニュ！　よおこそ、 ワタシのレストランへ！
English: Bienvenue! Welcome to my petite restauranté!

Japanese: ‥‥ぶにゅ、だって。
English: Huh? "Bee Avenue"?

Japanese: あらあらあら、アナタ。
English: Oh, non, my petite chulip!

Japanese: は、はい？　あたしですか？
English: Huh? Me?

Japanese: なんか、人生の負けイヌみたいな カオして。つかれてるのねえ。
English: Look at z'is face! Like la kitten rejected by its own mother. You are fatigued, non?

Japanese: そおゆうときは、これよお。
English: Alors! You need z'is!

Japanese: ネロリとローズのアロマバスが オ・ス・ス・メ。
English: An aromatic bath oil mélange of la néroli and la rose. My personal recommendation!

Japanese: あろまばす？
English: You think I need what...?

Japanese: このオイルを、おフロに ちょっと、しのばせてみて。
English: Oui, oui! Just add a couple of drops of z'is mixture to la bath water, and voilà!

Japanese: アナタのココロ、 いやしちゃうわよお。
English: It will soothe your body and your mind. It's simply fantastique!

Japanese: はああ‥‥。
English: Really?

Japanese: それから、アナタ！
English: And for la monsieur!

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくですか。
English: Who? Me?

Japanese: 死に神に見捨てられたみたいな カオして。つかれてるのねえ。
English: Look at z'at face! Like la puppy rejected by life itself! You are fatigued, non?

Japanese: そんなときは、こっちよお。
English: For you, monsieur, I recommend z'is!

Japanese: ベルガモットのオイル。 ‥‥それに、そおねえ。
English: Oil of bergamot. And maybe a 'int of... Oui, oui!

Japanese: ペパーミントとクラリセージも そえて、香水にしてあげましょ。
English: I will add la peppermint and la clary sage for a fragrance exceptionnel!

Japanese: エネルギーがあふれて、あなたの ウツクシサを引き出しちゃうから！
English: Such an invigorating recipe will bring out your delicious beauty, monsieur!

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくのウツクシサですか！
English: M-My beauty!?

Japanese: さ。じゃあ、席におつきになって。 ワタシのおすすめメニュウを‥‥
English: Alors. If you will be seated, I will bring you la special menu of z'e day!

Japanese: あ、あの。 ぼくたち、弁護士でして‥‥。
English: Actually, we're not here to eat. We're lawyers.

Japanese: ビアン・シュール！ モチロン、知ってるわあ。
English: Mais bien sûr! I know z'is already, monsieur.

Japanese: たしか‥‥ ナルホドーさん、でしょお？
English: You are la Phoenix Wright, non?

Japanese: え‥‥。ぼくのこと、 知ってるんですか？
English: Um, yes... You know me?

Japanese: 法廷で、ワタシをいぢめて くれちゃって。忘れないわよォ！
English: Mais, oui oui! I never forget a man who flirts wiz me! Especially in court!

Japanese: ニセなるほどくんの 尋問を受けたみたいだね‥‥
English: I guess he was cross-examined by our mysterious Xin Eohp...

Japanese: 事件の関係者は‥‥みんな “ぼく”を知ってるワケか‥‥
English: It looks like everyone to do with this case knows who "I" am already.

Japanese: どんなヤツなんだろうね。 ニセなるほどくん‥‥
English: I wonder what sort of impression Xin Eohp's been leaving on people, don't you?

Japanese: 本土坊　薫（ほんどぼうかおる）。 あらためて、よろしくネ！
English: Allow me to introduce myself to you again. I am Jean Armstrong. Enchanté!

Japanese: あの‥‥本土坊さん。
English: Um, Mr. Armstrong...

Japanese: 最後に、もう一度だけ カクニンさせてください。
English: Could I just confirm something with you again?

Japanese: 被害者が座っていた テーブルには‥‥
English: The table where the victim was sitting...

Japanese: 本当に、ヒトリしか いなかったんですね‥‥？
English: Was anyone else sitting there?

Japanese: ‥‥それなら、アナタたちが 自分で聞いてみなさいな。
English: Z'at is a question you will 'ave to ask 'im yourselves.
Huh? "'Im"?

Japanese: あのオジサンなら、いつも 公園にいるから。
English: La old man spends all of 'is time dans la parc.

Japanese: こうえん‥‥ ど、どこの公園ですか！
English: La p-parc? Oh, a park? What park's that?

Japanese: お店のウラにあるわ。 《ビタミン広場》ってトコ。
English: Behind la restaurant. It is called, "Vitamin Square".

Japanese: あ、ありがとうございます！
English: Thank you!

Japanese: ジュ・ヴザン・プリ！
English: Je vous en prie, my dear!

Japanese: さっそく、行ってみようよ！ ‥‥ビタミン広場。
English: Let's go check out this Vitamin Square right now, Nick!

Japanese: ああら。マドモワゼル！
English: Mademoiselle!

Japanese: はは、はい。
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: シゴトをお・さ・が・し？
English: Are you looking for la job?

Japanese: え！　いえ、その。 あたしは‥‥
English: What!? N-No, no. I was just...

Japanese: そうね。スタイルがちょっとアレ だけど、カオはまあまあだし‥‥
English: Let me see. Your style is un peu différent, but you 'ave a good face.

Japanese: “ちょっとアレ”？
English: ...Different?

Japanese: フェリシタシオン！　合格よ！ ウチで使ってあげましょ。
English: Félicitations! You 'ave passed! I will 'ire you!

Japanese: さ。さ。いらっしゃいな。
English: Bien! Come wiz me.

Japanese: ‥‥お姉さんが、 イチから教えてあげるから！
English: I will teach you everyz'ing I know!

Japanese: なな、なるほどくうん！ たーすけてぇ‥‥
English: N-Nick! HEEEEEELP!

Japanese: （‥‥奥のほうへ 引きずられていってしまった）
English: (I don't know whether to laugh or feel bad for Maya... Maybe I should do both...?)

Japanese: ファッション雑誌ばっかり。 しかも、フランス語だよ。
English: It's a rack full of fashion magazines. And they're all in French.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんも、たまには おしゃれしてみたら？
English: Why don't you try wearing something a bit more "chic" sometime, huh, Maya?

Japanese: そうだなあ。あたし、いっつも “装束”ばっかりだもんね。
English: Yeah. I guess I could. I'm always in my acolyte clothes, aren't I?

Japanese: たまにはフツーの お洋服もいいなあ。
English: It'd be fun to wear normal clothes every now and then.

Japanese: ‥‥？　ラックの後ろ‥‥、 何か挟まってるぞ。
English: Hm? There's something stuffed in behind the rack.

Japanese: ああ。これ？ ‥‥スポーツ新聞、てやつだね。
English: ...Looks like a sports paper to me.

Japanese: お。なんか、サインペンで ラクガキしてあるよ。
English: Hey, and look at this! Someone's scribbled a little doodle on one of the pages.

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》 いち、じゅう、ひゃく、せん‥‥
English: "MC Bomber", and one, two, three, four, five zeros...

Japanese: い、イッセンマン円‥‥、かな？
English: A-A hundred thousand... dollars... maybe?

Japanese: な、なんのことだろう。 《くりーにんぐ・ぼんばー》‥‥？
English: I wonder what "MC Bomber" is supposed to be...?

Japanese: （この新聞‥‥日付が、 １２月　３日‥‥）
English: (This paper! It's from December 3rd...)

Japanese: 毒殺事件が起こった日の新聞だ！
English: This paper's from the day of the poisoning!

Japanese: ええええええええッ！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: 《スポーツ新聞》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Sports Paper added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （事件当日のスポーツ新聞‥‥ 手がかりになるはずだ！）
English: (A paper from the day of the murder... This has got to be a clue!)

Japanese: （よく調べてみよう‥‥）
English: (I should see if I can find out some more about this paper...)

Japanese: ファッション雑誌ばっかり。 しかも、フランス語だよ。
English: It's a rack full of fashion magazines. And they're all in French.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんも、たまには おしゃれしてみたら？
English: Why don't you try wearing something a bit more "chic" sometime, huh, Maya?

Japanese: そうだなあ。たまには パリっぽくキメてみようかな‥‥
English: Yeah. I'd look stunning in some of those Parisian frocks, huh?

Japanese: （このラックで見つけた スポーツ新聞‥‥）
English: (We found that sports paper behind this magazine rack earlier...)

Japanese: （できるかぎり、 手がかりを探してみよう‥‥）
English: (I'd better try to find out everything I can about it...)

Japanese: ファッション雑誌ばっかり。 しかも、フランス語だよ。
English: It's a rack full of fashion magazines. And they're all in French.

Japanese: 新聞は置かないコトに してるらしいからな。
English: Looks like Mr. Armstrong doesn't get any newspapers.

Japanese: まあ、フンイキに 合わないからねー。
English: Well, it wouldn't exactly suit the feel of the place, would it?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんも、たまには おしゃれしてみたら？
English: Why don't you try wearing something a bit more "chic" like in these mags, Maya?

Japanese: そうだなあ。たまには パリっぽくキメてみようかな‥‥
English: Yeah. I'd look stunning in some of those Parisian frocks, huh?

Japanese: （このラックで見つけた スポーツ新聞‥‥）
English: (We found that sports paper behind this magazine rack earlier...)

Japanese: （できるかぎり、 手がかりを探してみよう‥‥）
English: (I'd better try to find out everything I can about it...)

Japanese: 入り口のドアだ。
English: It's the restaurant's front entrance.

Japanese: パネルがぶら下がっていて、 フランス語で何やら書いてある。
English: There's a sign hanging on the door written in French.

Japanese: 《営業中》《準備中》の どちらかだろう。
English: It probably says, "Open" or "Closed".

Japanese: どっちかわからないけど。 ‥‥フランス語だから。
English: It must be one or the other, but I don't know which since I dunno Jacques about French.

Japanese: なんか、カワイイ小物が ちんまり並んでるよ。
English: Look at all the little trinkets tucked away in here.

Japanese: きっと、店長さんの シュミなんだろうね。
English: I bet Mr. Armstrong collected all of these personally.

Japanese: ブーケやら、ポプリやら、 ボーンチャイナのカップやら‥‥
English: Let's see... A bouquet of flowers, some potpourri, and look! Fine bone china cups!

Japanese: なるほどくん、ナニ人？
English: I never knew you were so... cultured.

Japanese: これぐらい、ニッポン人でも 知ってるよ！
English: Come on, Maya! This is common knowledge! Any Joe Schmoe knows this much!

Japanese: いくらでもお金が出てくる、 魔法のハコがあるよ。
English: Look, it's one of those magical boxes that spits out money.

Japanese: ‥‥レジをそう表現するヒトは 初めてだな。
English: You know, you're the only person who would ever describe a cash register in that way!

Japanese: ‥‥事件があった テーブル、かな。
English: This must be the table where the murder occurred.

Japanese: 立ち入り禁止の テープが張ってあるよ‥‥。
English: I guess so, with all this police tape all around it.

Japanese: テーブルのシミは‥‥ 毒入りのコーヒーかな。
English: And that stain must be from the poisoned coffee.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。テーブルクロス、 ナメちゃダメだぞ。死ぬから。
English: Don't go licking the tablecloth, OK, Maya?

Japanese: ナメないよ！ あたしをナメないでよね！
English: Why would I lick it? I'm not a cat, you know!

Japanese: （お。ウマいコト言うな‥‥）
English: (Then why can I picture you doing just that?)

Japanese: キチンとセットされた テーブルだ。
English: This table's set nicely. It just needs a customer.

Japanese: お花がかざってあるよ。 ね。なんていうお花かな。
English: What do you think this flower is, Nick?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: Let's see...

Japanese: ひまわりでもチューリップでも ないみたいだね。
English: Well, it doesn't look like a tulip, and it's not a sunflower, I don't think...

Japanese: それぐらい、あたしでもわかるよ。
English: Duh. Even I could've told you that.

Japanese: それぐらいしかわからないんだ。 ぼくには。
English: Well, those are the only kinds of flowers I know. Dagnabit, I'm a lawyer, not a botanist!

Japanese: 窓のそとは、一面の銀世界だ。
English: Wow! It's a beautiful winter wonderland out there.

Japanese: え！　ウソ！ あたし、大好きなんだよ、雪。
English: Really!? Cool! I love snow! Let me see!

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。降ってないじゃない、 雪なんて。
English: Huh? It's not white. It's not even snowing.

Japanese: う、ウソに決まってるだろ。
English: Got you! I was only kidding, Maya.

Japanese: なるほどくんッ！
English: Nick!

Japanese: 言っていいウソと 悪いウソがあるよッ！
English: There are lies that are OK to tell, and lies that definitely aren't.

Japanese: （悪いウソだったのか‥‥今の）
English: (...All I did was tell an itty-bitty white lie about nonexistent white snow.)

Japanese: テーブルの間に、 仕切りが立っている。
English: This restaurant has partitions that separate the tables.

Japanese: 席について見えるのは、 向かい側のテーブルだけ、か。
English: When you're seated at a table, you can only see the tables to your right or left.

Japanese: あの。あの。《吐麗美庵》って どういう意味なんですか？
English: So what does "Trés Bien" mean?

Japanese: あたし、“庵”は わかるんですけど‥‥。
English: I know "trés". That means "three", right?

Japanese: ノンノンノン。“トレ・ビアン”は フランス語なのよ。
English: Non, non, non! "Trés Bien" is Français!

Japanese: 英語なら“ベリー・グー”。
English: In English, you would say, "Very Good".

Japanese: はああ。べりー・ぐー。
English: Oh. Very good...

Japanese: この店はね。イキでイナセで 本格的なフランス料理を出すの。
English: Oui, exactement! La atmosphere is trés bien, and la cuisine is trés bien!

Japanese: （本格的‥‥って、それにしては ゼンゼン客がいないな‥‥）
English: (If the food's so good, why aren't there any customers in here?)

Japanese: お口に合わないヒトもいるみたい。 ‥‥本格的な変化球だから。
English: My cuisine is not for all. Some people, z'ey do not appreciate la haute cuisine.

Japanese: （変化球かよ！）
English: (I thought everyone liked hot cuisine...)

Japanese: マコちゃんがいなくなっちゃって、 さすがに人手が足りないのよねえ。
English: Since I 'ave lost Maggey, I do not 'ave enough 'ands.

Japanese: あの。この店、おひとりで？
English: So, you're running this place on your own now?

Japanese: 今はそうね。アルバイトのコでも 来てくれないかしらねえ‥‥
English: Oui. For la moment. No one 'as answered my advertisement. Oh, poor moi!

Japanese: （‥‥こっちをジロジロ ながめている‥‥）
English: (...Please don't eyeball me while you say that.)

Japanese: ワタシ、シェフで店長なの。
English: I am la chef. I am la manager.

Japanese: 料理の他に、アロマテラピーも やってるのよお。
English: I am also a trained aromatherapist.

Japanese: あろま‥‥？
English: A roaming what?

Japanese: アロマテラピー。お花のカオリで ココロをいやす、ステキなシュミ。
English: A practitioner d'aromatherapy. La art of soothing la soul wiz z'e delicate floral aromas.

Japanese: （さっきの、キツいニオイの ビンのことか‥‥）
English: (Delicate? The smell coming from that bottle earlier was anything but...)

Japanese: 事件のこと、 聞かせてもらえますか？
English: So, could you tell me what you know about the incident?

Japanese: ‥‥エ・ビアン。ツラいけど、 まだハッキリおぼえてるわあ。
English: Bien. It makes me sad to remember it, yet I remember it so well.

Japanese: あれからもう、１ヶ月 たっちゃったのよねえ‥‥
English: More z'an a month 'as passed since it 'appened.

Japanese: （そうか‥‥。一度、有罪判決が 下されてるんだからな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I guess it's been about a month since Maggey's sentencing...)

Japanese: ‥‥あれは、先月の３日、 午後１時すぎだったわ。
English: So. It was z'e third of last month. Just after one in z'e afternoon.

Japanese: コーヒーを飲んだ男の人が、 急に苦しみだしたの。
English: A man who was in 'ere for a coffee suddenly became ill.

Japanese: たしか‥‥コーヒーに、 毒が入っていた、とか。
English: Because of the poison in his coffee?

Japanese: そうみたい。コーヒー、 マコちゃんが運んだんだけど。
English: Z'at is la truth as I know it. It was Maggey who took 'is drink to 'im.

Japanese: ワタシ、キッチンにいて‥‥ ヒトが倒れる音を聞いたわ。
English: I was in la kitchen. I 'eard z'e sound of someone collapsing.

Japanese: 見に行ったら‥‥ もう、グッタリしてて。
English: When I came out to see what it was, 'e was already slumped in 'is chair.

Japanese: 亡くなっていたんですか‥‥
English: He was dead?

Japanese: モン・デュ！　そのとおりよ！ マコちゃんも気を失っちゃって。
English: Mon dieu! Oui! 'E was dead! Maggey 'ad passed out also.

Japanese: あの。亡くなった“男の人”‥‥ ひとり、だったんですか？
English: And this man who died... Was he alone?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。 ヒトリだったわ。
English: Oui, monsieur. All alone.

Japanese: マコちゃんは‥‥“２人いた” って言ってたみたいだけど。
English: I know z'at Maggey said z'ere was someone else, but...

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: 警察にも、 何度も聞かれたけどねえ。
English: La police, z'ey asked me many times.

Japanese: 『テーブルに、もうヒトリ いませんでしたか』って。
English: "Are you sure z'ere was no one else at z'e table?" z'ey asked.

Japanese: でも‥‥ワタシだけじゃなくて、 あのオジサンも見ていないのよ。
English: But I am not z'e only one. La old man said z'e same thing.

Japanese: お、オジサン‥‥？
English: Old man? What old man?

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥ 《オジさん》というのは？
English: Um... So who is this old man that you mentioned?

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき‥‥ お客さんがもう１人、いたのよ。
English: At z'e time of la murder, z'ere was another customer in 'ere.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか！
English: What!? Someone else saw it?

Japanese: ウイ。常連さんでね。 あの日も来てたの。１人で。
English: Mais, oui! As usual, 'e came alone z'at day.

Japanese: そのヒトも、事件の瞬間を 目撃したんだけど‥‥
English: At z'e time of la murder, 'e was 'ere. 'E saw it, too.

Japanese: 死んだヒトのテーブルには、 他にダレもいなかった、って。
English: But 'e said z'e same thing, z'at z'ere was no one else at la victim's table.

Japanese: でも！　‥‥マコちゃんは “２人いた”って‥‥！
English: But Maggey swears there were two people!

Japanese: ザンネンだけど‥‥弁護士さん、 それを立証できなかったでしょ？
English: Mais, mademoiselle. La lawyer, 'e could not prove z'is, non?

Japanese: その“弁護士さん”、って‥‥ やっぱり、ぼくですか？
English: About the lawyer. That was me, I suppose?

Japanese: ‥‥メ・ビアン・シュール！
English: Mais, bien sûr!

Japanese: （どうやら、ちがうみたいだな）
English: Wow! He's the first person who's said it wasn't me.

Japanese: ダメだよ！　自分をゴマかしちゃ！
English: Don't kid yourself, Nick! Hmph. Now who's the one making stuff up!?

Japanese: マコちゃんが被害者に コーヒーを運んだとき‥‥
English: When Maggey took the coffee over to the victim...

Japanese: 何か、あったんですか？
English: ...did anything happen?

Japanese: う‥‥ウイ。 まあ、そうかしらね‥‥
English: Ou... Oui. I-I suppose you could say so.

Japanese: いったい‥‥何が？
English: So what happened?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ベツに。 たいしたコトじゃないわ。
English: Non. It was, er... It was nothing.

Japanese: マコちゃん、被害者のこと、 見たこともないって言ってます！
English: Look, Maggey says she didn't even know the guy!

Japanese: それなのに、殺人罪で 起訴されたってコトは‥‥
English: But she's still been indicted for murder!

Japanese: 検察側は、何か“動機”を 提示したはずです！
English: The prosecution must have come up with some kind of motive!

Japanese: それは‥‥そうだケド‥‥
English: Oui. It is true.

Japanese: 被害者との間に何かあったのなら、 それが動機と考えられたのかも‥‥
English: If there was anything at all between Maggey and the victim, it could be relevant.

Japanese: おねがいです！ ‥‥情報をください！
English: So please! Tell us anything you know.

Japanese: （サイコ・ロック‥‥か！）
English: (A Psyche-Lock!?)

Japanese: うえええ。 どうする？　なるほどくん。
English: No way! What are we going to do, Nick?

Japanese: そりゃ、解除するしか‥‥ あああああっ！
English: We'll just have to remov... WHAT THE!?

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしたの？
English: Wh-What's wrong?

Japanese: 勾玉が‥‥ない。
English: The Magatama... It's gone!
Huh?

Japanese: ポケットに入れておいたのに‥‥ いつのまにか、消えちまった！
English: I had it in my pocket, but... It has vanished into thin air!

Japanese: えええええええええッ！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: （‥‥でも、ロックが《見えた》 ということは‥‥）
English: (But I could see the Psyche-Lock.)

Japanese: （勾玉も、この近くにある、 ということか‥‥？）
English: (Maybe that means the Magatama's nearby...?)

Japanese: 見ず知らずのヒトのコーヒーに、 毒なんか入れませんよね？
English: Maggey wouldn't just have put poison in the coffee of some guy she didn't even know.

Japanese: マコちゃんと被害者のあいだに‥‥ いったい、何があったんですか？
English: If she's really guilty, there must have been something between her and the victim.

Japanese: き、きっとカンケイないわよ。 そんなコト。
English: N-Non. It was nothing. I am sure.

Japanese: ケ・セラ・セラ。‥‥他に 理由があったんじゃないかしら。
English: Qué será, será. Perhaps Maggey 'ad another reason.

Japanese: （‥‥マコちゃんに、何か 動機があったとして‥‥）
English: (What's Maggey's motive got to do with this chef?)

Japanese: （なぜ、このシェフが 必死にかくそうとするんだ？）
English: (Why's he hiding things from me?)

Japanese: （そして、もう１つ‥‥）
English: (And another thing...)

Japanese: （あの勾玉、どこで 落としちまったんだろう‥‥）
English: (Where did I drop the Magatama?)

Japanese: （ロックが見えるということは、 近くにあるはずなんだけど‥‥）
English: (The fact that I can see the lock must mean it's nearby.)

Japanese: あれ、貸してるだけだからね。
English: I only loaned it to you, you know.

Japanese: ちゃんと見つけるんだよ、 なるほどくん！
English: So you better find it, Nick! OK?

Japanese: （く、くれたんじゃ なかったのか‥‥）
English: (...And here I thought it was mine to keep!)

Japanese: そんなの見せなくても、 だいじょうぶよお。
English: Please, monsieur. Z'ere is no need to show me z'at.

Japanese: ムッシュ・ナルホドーでしょ。 ‥‥ウデの悪い弁護士さん。
English: You are Mon. Phoenix Wright, z'e worst defense lawyer in town.

Japanese: （しっかり、まちがって インプットされているな‥‥）
English: (I think I can imagine how he formed this completely wrong impression of me...)

Japanese: あの‥‥前に会ったときも、 このバッジ、つけてました？
English: Um, the last time we met, did I show you this badge?

Japanese: ウイ。こないだも 見せてたでしょ、それ。
English: Oui. You flashed it to everyone in la restaurant!

Japanese: ニセモノも、それを見せるのが 好きみたいだね。
English: Looks like Xin Eohp is a bigger fan of flashing stuff than you are, Nick!

Japanese: シロートのワタシが 言うのもアレだけど。
English: I am not a lawyer myself. I do not wish to speak out of turn.

Japanese: こないだの、あなたの弁護は どうかと思ったわあ。
English: But your defense in court z'at day was a little, how you say, "lacking", per'aps?

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: あれだったら、コトバの通じない フランス人のほうがマシね。
English: Even a Frenchman who cannot speak any English could 'ave done a better job!

Japanese: 見た目がカッコイイし。
English: You were very cool, though. Oh, oui, oui! So 'andsome.

Japanese: いったい、どんなブザマな 弁護をしたんだろうねえ。
English: Wow, I wonder just how bad the defense could've been...

Japanese: アナタが口を開くたび、 法廷がザワめいていたわぁ。
English: Every time you opened your mouth, z'e 'ole courthouse stirred.

Japanese: （うう‥‥想像したくない‥‥）
English: (Oh, man. That is something I don't want to imagine...)

Japanese: このスポーツ新聞‥‥ そこのラックにあったんですけど。
English: We found this sports paper in the magazine rack here.

Japanese: お客さんが置いていったみたい。 捨て忘れてたのね。
English: One of my customers must 'ave left it behind.

Japanese: だれが置いていったか、 ココロあたり、ありませんか？
English: Do you have any idea which customer it was?

Japanese: ワタシが関心があるのは、 食あたりだけなのよお。
English: Z'e only ideas I 'ave, mademoiselle, I save for my kitchen!

Japanese: ‥‥オー・ラ・ラ。アナタ、 とってもいいエガオじゃない！
English: Oh là là! You 'ave such a pretty smile, my petite chulip!

Japanese: え！　そうですか？
English: Who, me?

Japanese: やっぱり、アタシたち女性は エガオがダイジよねえ！
English: Oui. La smile is the most important thing for us girls, n'est-ce pas?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですよね。
English: Um... Sure!

Japanese: アルバイトがしたくなったら いつでも、おいでなさいな。
English: If you want a job 'ere, it is yours, precious!

Japanese: マドモアゼル・マヨイ。 お店のほうはどう？
English: Mademoiselle Maya! 'Ow are you finding my restaurant?

Japanese: ええ。だいぶ なれてきましたけど。
English: I think I've got the hang of it now...

Japanese: まあ、今回は これぐらいで‥‥
English: But I think it's time for me to...

Japanese: オー・ラ・ラ！ もう、やめちゃうの？
English: Oh là là, non! You are quitting already?

Japanese: アルバイトの最短キロクを 大きくぬりかえたわね。
English: It will be a new record for z'e shortest period of employment ever!

Japanese: モン・デュ！　ステキな マドモワゼルね‥‥！
English: Mon dieu! What beauty!

Japanese: あ！　あたしの お姉ちゃんなんですよ！
English: That's my sister!

Japanese: あらあら。セ・ラ・ヴィ！ そおねえ‥‥
English: Ah, oui? Another delight!

Japanese: アナタにも、お姉さんには ないミリョクが、きっとあるわ。
English: But you 'ave a certain je ne sais quois z'at I do not sense from your sister.

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: I do?

Japanese: だから、ムネを張って 生きていきなさいな。
English: So throw your 'ead 'igh! Thrust out your feminine features!

Japanese: くじけちゃダメよ！ オーエス！　オーエス！
English: Do not lose 'eart, ma fille! You are a woman! A woman extraordinaire!

Japanese: ‥‥なんで、なぐさめられなきゃ ならないんだろ。
English: Do I look like I need cheering up or something?

Japanese: マコちゃん‥‥婦人警官 だったんですってねえ？
English: Maggey was a policewoman once, n'est-ce pas?

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと事情があって、 やめちゃったみたいですけど。
English: Yes. But she had to quit for, um, reasons beyond her control.

Japanese: 聞いてるわあ。サツジンの 疑いをかけられたんでしょ？
English: Oui, oui. She was la suspect in la murder investigation, non?

Japanese: し、知ってたんですか‥‥
English: Oh... You know about that?

Japanese: だからね。ワタシ、シアワセになる 香水をあげたワケ。
English: Z'at is why I gave to 'er la perfume for la 'appiness.

Japanese: シアワセに‥‥？
English: Happiness perfume?

Japanese: ウイ。さっきあなたにあげた、 ベルガモットの。
English: Oui. Blended from bergamot, like I 'ave given to you before.

Japanese: でも‥‥彼女、またタイホされて 今度は有罪ですよ。
English: But she's been arrested again. And found guilty this time.

Japanese: あの子の不幸のアロマのほうが キツかった、ってコトかしら。
English: Z'is is true. 'Er natural aroma of un'appiness must 'ave been very strong.

Japanese: （なんだよ！　効かない じゃないか、この香水！）
English: (Just admit it! Your perfume doesn't work!)

Japanese: まあ‥‥疑われても、 しかたないかもしれないわね。
English: I am not surprised she was la prime suspect.

Japanese: 目の前で、あんなコトが 起こっちゃったらねえ‥‥
English: After something like z'at took place before my very eyes.

Japanese: “あんなコト”‥‥？
English: Something like what?

Japanese: （ど、どういうコトだ‥‥！）
English: (What's this guy talking about!?)

Japanese: もしかして‥‥
English: Does this mean...

Japanese: マコちゃんに‥‥“動機があった” っていうコト、かな‥‥
English: Maggey did have a motive?

Japanese: く、詳しく聞いてみよう！
English: We've got to ask this guy for more info, stat!

Japanese: こう見えても、パリのお店で ５年間、みっちり修行したのよォ。
English: It is 'ard to imagine, I know, but I was an apprentice for five years in la gay Paris.

Japanese: へええ。スゴいですねー！
English: Wow! In Paris!?

Japanese: そのお店のシェフのヒトコトで、 自分のお店を開く決心をしたの。
English: It was something la chef z'ere said to me z'at made me decide to open my own restaurant.

Japanese: なんて言われたんですか？
English: What was it?

Japanese: 『あと１０年修行が必要だ』って。
English: Z'at awful man said to me, "You must train for another ten years!"

Japanese: やってられないでしょ？　だから 帰ってきちゃったのよねえ。
English: Oh! It was such a shock! So unexpected! But you would 'ave to be a fool to do z'is, non?

Japanese: これなんですけど‥‥
English: Um, about this...

Japanese: フェリシタシオン！ メ‥‥ケスク・セ？
English: Félicitations! Mais... qu'est-ce que c'est?

Japanese: え‥‥！ み‥‥、みー・とぅー。
English: Ack! Um... Je... Je no comprehend...

Japanese: なるほどくん。 テキトーに返事しない！
English: Nick! Don't just make something up!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 レストラン　吐麗美庵
English: January 6 Trés Bien

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥あいかわらず キツいな、花のカオリ‥‥
English: The scent of flowers sure is strong. It's almost making me dizzy...

Japanese: あ‥‥ ど、どうも‥‥
English: Oh, um... Hello.

Japanese: （な、なんだ今の‥‥ お客さん、か？）
English: (Who was that just now? A customer?)

Japanese: （ミョーにしめっぽい 感じのヒトだったな‥‥）
English: (She had sort of a dark aura about her...)

Japanese: あ！　いらっしゃいませー。
English: Ah! Welcome! Bee avenue!

Japanese: （お。かわいらしい声‥‥）
English: (Wow. What a cute voice.)

Japanese: なーんだ、なるほどくんかあ。
English: Oh, it's just you, Nick.

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃん！
English: M-Maya!

Japanese: どうかな。似合う？
English: Well? How do I look?

Japanese: 霊媒師、やめたほうが いいかもしれないね。
English: Maybe you should quit being a spirit medium...

Japanese: それにしても、ヒマだねー。 ウエイトレスさん。
English: Maybe. But it's kind of boring being a waitress.

Japanese: お客さん、なるほどくんが 初めてだもん。
English: I mean, you're my first ever customer.

Japanese: （あれ。さっきのジメッとした 女のヒトは‥‥？）
English: (Then who was that woman I just saw?)

Japanese: あ。そうだ！
English: Oh, oh!

Japanese: せっかくだから、 なんか食べていってよ！
English: Since you're here, you might as well have something to eat!

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、 おなかへったな‥‥
English: I am kind of hungry, actually.

Japanese: ‥‥ところで、ランチだけど‥‥
English: Um... About the lunch...

Japanese: おッ！　カクゴを決めたね！
English: Oh! A fine choice, sir.

Japanese: え。いや‥‥
English: No. I, um...

Japanese: ちゅーもん入りましたあ！
English: KITCHEN! A LUNCH SPECIAL, PLEASE!

Japanese: ランチワン！　ドリンク、サラダ、 デザートにおみやげつきでー！
English: WITH ALL THE EXTRAS: DRINK, SIDE SALAD, DESSERT, AND GIFT!

Japanese: そ、そんなにいらないよ！
English: I don't need any of that!

Japanese: しょーしょー お待ちくださあい！
English: Just a moment, please, sir.

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃん、 うきうきして行っちまった）
English: (Maya's really getting into this!)

Japanese: （えーと‥‥ランチセット、 ２９８０円か）
English: (So how much is this set lunch, then? 20 dollars, huh?)

Japanese: （ドリンク、サラダ、デザートつき ‥‥‥‥ろ、６４００円？）
English: (But with the drink, side salad, and dessert, it's... 45 dollars!?)

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと！　真宵ちゃあん！
English: Hey, wait a sec! Maya!

Japanese: お待たせしましたあ！ こちら、ムシレセットでえす！
English: Sorry to keep you waiting, sir! Here you are, our deluxe Fortify lunch set!

Japanese: わ！
English: Whoa!

Japanese: オマールえびとアワビのフリカッセ バルサミコ酢風味でございまあす！
English: A dish inspired by lobster and abalone fricassée with a balsamic vinaigrette.

Japanese: ごゆっくり、めしあがれ！
English: Bon appétit!

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Um, thanks...?

Japanese: ほらほら。早くめしあがって みてよ、なるほどくん！
English: Come on, Nick! Hurry up and try it already!

Japanese: （オマールえび‥‥ねえ。 どれどれ‥‥）
English: (Lobster, huh? Alright... Down the hatch it goes...)
Urp!

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: Well?

Japanese: ‥‥ま、真宵ちゃん。 おなかへってない？
English: Are you hungry, Maya?

Japanese: うん。ぺこぺこ！
English: I'm starving!

Japanese: はい。あげる。
English: Here. It's yours.

Japanese: ホント！
English: Really!?
Urp!

Japanese: のこさず食べるんだよ。
English: Remember, Maya. My wallet doesn't print money, so you'd better polish off that plate!

Japanese: あ。あたしちょっと、 トイレ掃除してくるから。
English: I-I've just remembered! I've got to clean the toilets!

Japanese: おい！
English: Hey!

Japanese: （こりゃ、はやらないワケだ‥‥）
English: (You can't be in that much of a hurry to clean the toilets!)

Japanese: 吐麗美庵《ランチ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Trés Bien Lunch Special added to the Court Record.

Japanese: どうやったら、こんな マズい料理を作れるんだろう。
English: How does that guy manage to make good food taste so bad?

Japanese: ちょっと、のぞいてみる？ ‥‥キッチン。
English: Hey, Nick... You want to take a peek at the kitchen?

Japanese: （キッチン‥‥か‥‥）
English: (The kitchen, huh? Not a bad idea.)

Japanese: ラックには、ファッション雑誌が ズラリと並んでいる。
English: It's a rack full of fashion magazines. And they're all in French.

Japanese: ‥‥トンでもないデザインの 服ばっかりだ。
English: Ugh. All the clothes in these are absolutely atrocious!

Japanese: ところどころに、サインペンで 赤くマークされているけど‥‥
English: ...Some of them have been circled in red.

Japanese: もしかして、着るつもりなのか？ あの店長さん‥‥
English: I really hope Mr. Armstrong is not thinking of buying those.

Japanese: かわいらしい小物が ごちゃごちゃと並んでいる。
English: There's a bunch of little trinkets on a shelf by the door.

Japanese: きっと、店長さんの シュミなのだろう。
English: I bet Mr. Armstrong collected all of these personally.

Japanese: せまいスペースに レジが置いてある。
English: It's really tight behind the cash register counter.

Japanese: ‥‥入れるのか？ あの店長さん。
English: Can Mr. Armstrong even get in there?

Japanese: そして、出られるのか？ あの店長さん。
English: And if he got in... could he get out again?

Japanese: 事件があったテーブルだろう。 コーヒーのシミが残っている。
English: This must be the table where the poisoning occurred. The stain tells the story well.

Japanese: 立入禁止のテープが 張ってある、ということは‥‥
English: The whole area is still cordoned off with police tape.

Japanese: まだ捜査中、なのかな。
English: I guess it must still be under investigation.

Japanese: 店長さん、キレイ好きなのだろう。 キチンとセットされている。
English: Mr. Armstrong must be a pretty big neat-freak. He already has this table ready to go.

Japanese: これで、料理の味がよければ カンペキなのだが‥‥。
English: Now if only the food in this place was edible...

Japanese: 窓のそとには冬の街が見える。
English: It looks pretty cold out there on the streets.

Japanese: お正月あけなので、 のんびりしたムードだ。
English: Peaceful, though. It's nice that people can take it easy after the holiday rush.

Japanese: どう？　ウエイトレスの感想は。
English: So, how do you like your new job, Maya?

Japanese: シゴトはね。たくさん ありそうだよ。
English: I never knew there was so much for a waitress to do!

Japanese: 注文聞いて、お料理運んで、 コーヒーいれて、レジ打って。
English: Take people's orders, bring them their food, make coffee, work the cash register...

Japanese: ‥‥でも、お客さんが いれば、の話だけど。
English: Of course, we need a customer before I can do any of that.

Japanese: キレイな店なのに、ここまで 客が入らないとはね。
English: Yeah, it's a nice-looking restaurant. It's a shame more people don't come.

Japanese: かわいいウエイトレスさんも いるのにねー。
English: Don't forget about the ultra cute waitress!

Japanese: どう？　どう？　このカンペキな 営業スマイル。
English: Check out my "gimme a tip" smile!

Japanese: ねえねえ、何か 注文してよー、お料理。
English: Hey, Nick! Why don't you order something?

Japanese: 今なら、ランチセットが おトクらしいよ。ウワサでは。
English: The chef's preparing a tasty lunch set at the moment. Or so he says.

Japanese: それ、いくら？
English: How much is it?

Japanese: ２９８０円。 ニクハチセットっていうんだって。
English: It's the Twin-T set. So it's 20 dollars, of course.

Japanese: “ニクハチ”‥‥？ やめとくよ。高いし。
English: The Twin-T set? I believe I'll be taking a pass. It's kind of expensive.

Japanese: ええー！　せっかく ウエイトレスになったんだから、
English: What!? But you can't! Come on, Nick! It's not every day I get to be a waitress!

Japanese: あたしだって、お料理運んだり、 レジも打ったりしたいよー！
English: I want to try carrying plates and working the cash register!

Japanese: とりあえず、トイレ掃除でも してたら？
English: How about cleaning the toilets? That should keep you busy.

Japanese: それはいいや。
English: Yeah, right! Maybe later.

Japanese: あの。このランチだけど‥‥
English: So, about this lunch...

Japanese: おッ！　もういっちょ、 いっとく？　なるほどくん！
English: Oh! You want another one?

Japanese: いくワケないだろ！ この値段であの味はないよ！
English: Get real, Maya! For something that tastes like that, the price tag's a joke!

Japanese: ニクハチセットだもんねー。
English: But it's the Twin-T set. The price is kind of fixed.

Japanese: しかも、ぼくが食べたの ６４００円だろ？
English: I thought my set was 45 dollars, though.

Japanese: まあね。そっちは ムシレセットだから。
English: That's because you had the deluxe Fortify set.

Japanese: こりゃ、お客が入らなくて トーゼンだよ。
English: You know... I can see why no one ever comes in here!

Japanese: そうだねー。店長さん、 わかってないのかな‥‥？
English: Yeah. I wonder if the chef realizes what the problem is.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、マコちゃんが 言ってたっけ。
English: Hmm, now what was it that Maggey said again?

Japanese: キッチンに、ちょっと おもしろいものがある、って。
English: "In the kitchen, you'll get to see all of the chef's greatest secrets!"

Japanese: キッチンに？　ほおほお、 それは耳よりな情報だね‥‥
English: In the kitchen? Mmm. That sounds tasty!

Japanese: あ、ちょっとコラ、 真宵ちゃん！
English: Hey, wait up! Maya!

Japanese: なに？　あたし、 いそがしいんだから！
English: What is it? I'm pretty busy right now.

Japanese: いやいや。 ぼくも連れてってくれよ。
English: Weren't you going to show me around?

Japanese: ちぇ。おもしろいモノ見つけて、 見せびらかそうと思ったのに‥‥。
English: Tsk. There goes my plan to find some cool clue and show it off in your face...

Japanese: （キッチン‥‥ 調べてみよう！）
English: (I'd better conduct the search in the kitchen myself!)

Japanese: これなんだけど‥‥
English: About this...

Japanese: ゴメン、なるほどくん！
English: Sorry, Nick!

Japanese: あたし今、ウエイトレスさんの 仕事おぼえるのでタイヘンだから。
English: I'm a waitress now. I've got a pile of work waiting for little ol' me.

Japanese: あ。このオジさん‥‥。 まだ、エサやってるの？　ハトに。
English: Oh, it's that old man... Is he still feeding the pigeons?

Japanese: ぼくも食らわされたよ。
English: Yeah. He "fed" me as well. I got a bunch of those seeds in my eyes.

Japanese: ひゃあ‥‥
English: Ooh, ouch!

Japanese: ところで‥‥ちょっと 来てくれないかな。いっしょに。
English: Hey, Maya. Would you mind coming with me for a while?

Japanese: え。あたし？
English: Huh? Me? Why?

Japanese: どうしても、話を聞きたいんだよ。 あのオジさんから。
English: There's something I really want to ask that old man.

Japanese: うん、いいよ。 じゃ、着がえてくるから‥‥
English: Sure, OK. I'll just get changed.

Japanese: あ。ちょっと待って。
English: No, hang on!

Japanese: できれば‥‥ そのままがいいんだけど。
English: Can't you... go like that?

Japanese: ？　そうなんだ。
English: I guess...?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 レストラン　吐麗美庵
English: January 6 Trés Bien

Japanese: そろそろ、今日の調査も オシマイかねえ。
English: I guess it's about time to wrap up today's investigation.

Japanese: もう、いいの？ ウエイトレスさんは。
English: Had enough of being a waitress?

Japanese: うん。お客さん、来ないし。
English: Yeah. Plus, no one came to the restaurant...

Japanese: ああら！　マドモワゼル・マヨイ！ ダメでしょ。お店すっぽかしちゃ。
English: Oh là là! Mademoiselle Maya! Non! 'Ow can you leave me like z'is!?

Japanese: あ。す、すみません‥‥。
English: I-I'm sorry...

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (That reminds me.)

Japanese: （このヒトも、サイコ・ロックを 抱えていたんだっけ‥‥）
English: (Mr. Armstrong had a Psyche-Lock or three, didn't he?)

Japanese: （最後に、ソイツを 解除しておかないとな‥‥）
English: (I'm going to have to break those...)

Japanese: 本土坊さん。
English: Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: もう一度、お話を うかがいたいのですが‥‥
English: I hope you won't mind, but I'd like to have another word with you...

Japanese: ヴォロンティエ！ いいわよお。
English: Volontiers! Of course!

Japanese: ファッション雑誌ばっかり。 しかも、フランス語だよ。
English: It's a rack full of fashion magazines. And they're all in French.

Japanese: 新聞は置かないコトに してるらしいからな。
English: Looks like Mr. Armstrong doesn't get any newspapers.

Japanese: まあ、フンイキに 合わないからねー。
English: Well, it wouldn't exactly suit the feel of the place, would it?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんも、たまには おしゃれしてみたら？
English: Why don't you try wearing something a bit more "chic" like in these mags, Maya?

Japanese: そうだなあ。たまには パリっぽくキメてみようかな‥‥
English: Yeah. I'd look stunning in some of those Parisian frocks, huh?
(We found that sports paper behind this magazine rack earlier...)
(I'd better try to find out everything I can about it...)

Japanese: マコちゃんが被害者に コーヒーを運んだとき‥‥
English: When Maggey took the coffee over to the victim...

Japanese: いったい、何があったんですか？
English: ...did anything happen?

Japanese: そ‥‥それは‥‥
English: M-Monsieur... I cannot...

Japanese: 見ず知らずのヒトのコーヒーに、 毒なんか入れませんよね？
English: Maggey wouldn't just have put poison in the coffee of some guy she didn't even know.

Japanese: 被害者との間に何かあったのなら、 それが動機と考えられたのかも‥‥
English: If she's really guilty, there must have been something between her and the victim.

Japanese: き、きっとカンケイないわよ。 そんなコト。
English: N-Non. It was nothing. I am sure.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥）
English: (Somehow...)

Japanese: （この店長さんのヒミツを 聞き出すときが来たみたいだな）
English: (I've gotta get this guy to spill the beans on everything he knows.)

Japanese: その、宝くじのコトですけど‥‥
English: Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: そもそも、どうして あなたが知っていたんですか？
English: You said that the victim had a winning lottery ticket for half a million dollars.

Japanese: 被害者が、５０００万円の 当たりクジを持っていたって。
English: How did you know he had something like that in the first place?

Japanese: あのお客さん、ラジオ聞いてたの。 ‥‥イヤホンで。
English: Z'e man... 'E was listening to la radio wi'z 'is earpiece.

Japanese: （そういえば、マコちゃんも 言ってたな‥‥イヤホンのこと）
English: (Hmm, Maggey said something about that, too.)

Japanese: ‥‥宝クジの当選番号の ニュースを聞いていたみたい。
English: La winning number was announced on la news, I think.

Japanese: いきなり叫びだしたわ。 『当たった！　５０００万だッ！』
English: All of a sudden, 'e exploded! "Yes! 'Alf a million!" 'e shouted.

Japanese: そこに、宝クジが‥‥？
English: And the ticket?

Japanese: そう。テーブルの上に 広げてあったワケ。
English: Oui. 'E 'ad all of 'is tickets spread out on la table.

Japanese: わ‥‥ワタシ‥‥どうしても、 おカネが必要だった。だから‥‥
English: I... I was so desperately in need of money, so I...

Japanese: 《毒》を入れたワケですか。 ‥‥被害者のコーヒーに‥‥。
English: Put the poison in his coffee?

Japanese: ののの、のんのんのん！ チガうわよッ！
English: Non, non, non, non, non! Oh, non, you naughty man!

Japanese: ワタシはただ、クジを ちょっと１枚、もらっただけ。
English: I simply 'elped myself to one of 'is tickets.
What?

Japanese: コーヒーを飲んだ被害者が倒れて、 マコちゃんがキゼツして‥‥
English: La victim collapsed, and Maggey passed out.

Japanese: １枚ぐらい、いいかな、って。 いっぱいあるんだし。
English: I thought to myself, "Pourquoi pas?" 'E 'ad so many of zem.

Japanese: （当たりクジは１枚だけだぞ‥‥）
English: (Yeah, but only one of them was the winning ticket, right?)

Japanese: でも、店長さん！ ヒドいじゃないですか！
English: How could you do that, Mr. Armstrong!?

Japanese: そのせいで、マコちゃんは タイホされちゃったんですよッ！
English: Maggey was arrested because of you!

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ ちがうのよォッ！
English: Non! Z'is is not true!

Japanese: ワタシ‥‥盗んでないの！ ご、５０００万円のクジなんて！
English: I did not take it! La ticket for 'alf a million, I mean.

Japanese: だって今、言ったじゃないですか！ “１枚もらった”‥‥って！
English: But you just told us you did! You said you took a ticket!

Japanese: だ。だから。それは‥‥‥
English: Mais non, ma fille! It was not...

Japanese: ‥‥そこまでだ。
English: That's enough.
Huh?

Japanese: あああああああああッ！
English: AAAAAAAAAH!

Japanese: ゴドー検事ィィィィッ！
English: Mr. Godot!

Japanese: な、ナニしてるんですか、 こんなトコロで‥‥！
English: What in the heck are you doing here!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
English: Urgh!

Japanese: こんなにすっぱいコーヒー‥‥ 初めてだぜ、マスター。
English: This is without doubt... the worst coffee I have ever tasted, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: （コーヒー飲んでたのかよ！）
English: (He came in here for coffee!? Does his craving for coffee know no bounds!?)

Japanese: 本土坊は‥‥たしかにあの日、 被害者のクジを盗んじまった。
English: Perhaps Mr. Armstrong stole one of the victim's tickets on the day in question.

Japanese: バカなオンナよ！ 笑ってやってちょうだいな！
English: I am la air'ead, non? Just a pretty little girl who everyone is laughing at.

Japanese: でも！　それなら、アヤシイのは マコちゃんだけじゃなくて‥‥
English: But, in that case, Maggey shouldn't be the only one under suspicion...

Japanese: 番号をまちがえたのさ。
English: He had the wrong ticket.
...What?

Japanese: 本土坊は‥‥となりのクジを シッケイした。当たりクジの、な。
English: Mr. Armstrong made off with the winning ticket's pretty neighbor.

Japanese: そ、それって‥‥ ハズレ、ってコトですか？
English: So... the ticket he took was worthless?

Japanese: いちおう、当たったらしい。 ‥‥１００円。
English: Not quite. He did win something. A dollar.

Japanese: ‥‥バカなオンナなのよぅ！ 笑ってってばあ！
English: You see! I am just a pretty face! W'izout my looks, I 'ave nothing!

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥
English: So...

Japanese: ホンモノの当たりクジは‥‥ どうなったんですか？
English: What happened to the winning ticket, then? The one he meant to steal.

Japanese: さあ。どうなったのかねえ‥‥
English: Indeed. What did happen to it?

Japanese: オレのルールでは、映画の予告は 見ないことにしている。
English: I don't like spoiling myself by watching trailers, so...

Japanese: お楽しみは、明日に とっておくことだ‥‥
English: ...we'll just wait and see how the movie turns out tomorrow, won't we?

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Nngh...

Japanese: ねええ！　そこの２人！ 笑ってってばあ！
English: Voilà! You two! Time to laugh at la pretty little air'ead!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥この紙きれ、 もういらないな‥‥）
English: (Looks like I won't be needing this note anymore.)

Japanese: 《五十嵐のメモ》を ゴミバコに捨てた。
English: Victor's Note thrown into the trash.

Japanese: ‥‥５０００万円の 当たりクジの行方、か‥‥
English: Looks like we've got a new mystery now. Namely, where did the winning ticket go?

Japanese: なんか、イヤな予感がするね。
English: I've got a bad feeling about this.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥
English: Well, anyway.

Japanese: マコちゃんに、２回もツラい 思いをさせるわけにはいかないよ。
English: We can't let Maggey suffer any longer for this. And certainly not again.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: ああら。あなたも シゴトをお・さ・が・し？
English: Oh là là! You are looking for la job also?

Japanese: ち、ちがいますよ！
English: N-No!

Japanese: そうね。カオがちょっとアレだけど スタイルはまあまあだし‥‥
English: Oui, oui. Your looks are un peu, unusual, shall we say? But I love your style!

Japanese: “ちょっとアレ”？
English: I look "unusual"?

Japanese: あなたなら、きっと似合うわ！ ウエイトレスの制服。
English: It will suit you perfectly, la uniform of la waitress!

Japanese: （そう来ると思った‥‥）
English: (I just knew it...)

Japanese: オー・ラ・ラ！
English: Oh là là! So you 'ave eaten my lunch, oui?

Japanese: いかがだったかしら？ ウチのランチ。
English: Tell me, monsieur. Did you enjoy la lunch I prepared for you?

Japanese: ‥‥個性的で、野性的な味でした。
English: It was a... unique and... wild mixture of flavors.

Japanese: オマールえび、って あたし、初めて食べました！
English: That was the first time I ever had lobster, you know!

Japanese: オマールえび‥‥？ そんなの、使ってないケド‥‥
English: Lobster? Z'ere was no lobster in my lunch...

Japanese: え！　だって、メニューに そう書いてありましたよ。
English: Huh? But, it said, "lobster" right on the menu.

Japanese: マドモワゼル・マヨイ。 メニューはちゃんと読んで。
English: Mademoiselle Maya. Please, you must read la menu more carefully.

Japanese: オマールえびとアワビのフリカッセ バルサミコ酢風味の、“何か”。
English: It says, "A dish inspired by lobster and abalone fricassée with a balsamic vinaigrette."

Japanese: な、“何か”‥‥ですかあ。
English: Oh. It's only inspired, is it?

Japanese: 最後に“風味”とつけてる ところがミソなのよお。
English: Oui. Inspired by z'ese ingredients, but not per'aps made from z'em, n'est-ce pas?

Japanese: こりゃ、いっぱい食わされたね、 なるほどくん。
English: Well, guess that's the way the cookie crumbles, Nick.

Japanese: （‥‥いったい、 ナニを食わされたんだ！）
English: (The way the lunch tasted, it's probably 99% perspiration and 1% inspiration.)

Japanese: ごめんなさあい。
English: Pardon, pardon.

Japanese: 老眼鏡がないと読めないのよぉ。 ジャポネ‥‥ニホン語。
English: W'izout my reading glasses, I cannot see z'is. I do not read English well.

Japanese: 見向きもしないよ。
English: He's not even looking at it.

Japanese: （使い方を考えたほうが よさそうだな‥‥）
English: (We better find a way to make him look, then.)

Japanese: マコちゃん‥‥婦人警官 だったんですってねえ？
English: Maggey was a policewoman once, n'est-ce pas?

Japanese: ちょっと事情があって、 やめちゃったみたいですけど。
English: Yes. But she had to quit for, um, reasons beyond her control.

Japanese: 聞いてるわあ。サツジンの 疑いをかけられたんでしょ？
English: Oui, oui. She was la suspect in la murder investigation, non?

Japanese: し、知ってたんですか‥‥
English: Oh... You know about that?

Japanese: だからね。ワタシ、シアワセになる 香水をあげたワケ。
English: Z'at is why I gave to 'er la perfume for la 'appiness.

Japanese: シアワセに‥‥？
English: Happiness perfume?

Japanese: ウイ。さっきあなたにあげた、 ベルガモットの。
English: Oui. Blended from bergamot, like I 'ave given to you before.

Japanese: でも‥‥彼女、またタイホされて 今度は有罪ですよ。
English: But she's been arrested again. And found guilty this time.

Japanese: あの子の不幸のアロマのほうが キツかった、ってことかしら。
English: Z'is is true. 'Er natural aroma of un'appiness must 'ave been very strong.

Japanese: （なんだよ！　効かない じゃないか、この香水！）
English: (Just admit it! Your perfume doesn't work!)

Japanese: ‥‥亡くなったカタね。 初めてのお客さんだったわ。
English: Ah, la dead man. 'E was la first in my restaurant.

Japanese: え！　記念すべき お客さん第１号‥‥
English: No way! You mean, the first customer you ever had!?

Japanese: そうじゃなくて‥‥
English: Non, non, non.

Japanese: 吐麗美庵に来たのが 初めてだったの。
English: It was z'e first time I've 'ad a dead man in my restaurant.

Japanese: ‥‥オキノドクね。
English: Such a s'ame to lose a customer after only 'is first meal 'ere!

Japanese: あら。このオジさんなら 常連さんよォ。
English: Ah, z'is old man. 'E is a regular 'ere.

Japanese: ウチのジマンのコーヒーと お冷やが大好きなの。
English: My specialty of cappuccino and cold water is one of 'is favorites.

Japanese: あの事件が起こったときも いたんですよね？
English: Was he here at the time of the incident?

Japanese: そうだったわねェ。 たしかに、いたわ。
English: Oui. 'E was 'ere. I am sure of it.

Japanese: あの事件が起こったときに、ね‥‥
English: When la incident 'appened, z'e old man was 'ere.

Japanese: ～マコの動機～
English: -- Maggey's Motive --

Japanese: なな、ナニよ？ この、異様なフンイキは‥‥
English: Wh-What is 'appening? I do not like zis 'orrible feeling.

Japanese: ‥‥なんとしても聞き出します。 あの日、何があったか‥‥？
English: I have to know the truth. What happened that day?

Japanese: わわ、わかったワ！ コワいから、白状しちゃう！
English: Alors, alors! I will confess everything! Just don't 'urt me!

Japanese: ‥‥え。 （い、いきなりかよ！）
English: Huh? (Well that was a new world record!)

Japanese: 宝クジなのよッ！
English: It was a lottery ticket.

Japanese: たからくじ‥‥？
English: A lottery ticket?

Japanese: あの、死んだ被害者のヒト‥‥ まちがいなく、持ってたの。
English: La man who died 'ere 'ad a lottery ticket.

Japanese: ５０００万円の当たりクジを！
English: For 'alf a million dollars!

Japanese: ごご‥‥５０００万円ですって！
English: H-HALF A MILLION!?

Japanese: その当たりクジだけが‥‥
English: Oui. But after la incident, z'is ticket...

Japanese: 事件が起こった直後に、 コツゼンと消えちゃったの！
English: It disappeared!

Japanese: （宝クジが‥‥“消えた”‥‥）
English: (The ticket disappeared...?)

Japanese: 裁判では、それが動機だって 言われてたわ！
English: Z'is was la motive z'at la prosecution gave for Maggey.

Japanese: あの子が、５０００万円のクジを 奪うために毒を盛った、って‥‥
English: Z'ey said z'at she poisoned the man to get ze 'alf a million dollar lottery ticket.

Japanese: ‥‥どうして、今まで 教えてくれなかったんですか？
English: Why didn't you tell me about this sooner?

Japanese: え！　そ、それは‥‥
English: Mais, alors...

Japanese: あなたは、宝クジのことを ぼくにかくそうとしていた‥‥
English: You've been trying to hide this information about the lottery ticket from me.

Japanese: そこには、何か“理由”が あったはずです！
English: And I want to know the reason why!

Japanese: な、何を言うのよォ！ せっかく教えてあげたのにィィ！
English: Non, monsieur! You doubt me? But I 'ave confessed to you everything I know!

Japanese: 本土坊さん‥‥
English: Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: ５０００万円の宝くじ。 被害者から、それを盗んだのは‥‥
English: The half a million dollar lottery ticket... I think I know who took it.

Japanese: もしかして、 この人物だったのでは‥‥？
English: I think the winning ticket was stolen by this person.

Japanese: さあ、どうですか！
English: Well? How about it?

Japanese: サ・ヴァ？
English: Ça va?

Japanese: は？
English: Excuse me?

Japanese: “お元気？”っていうイミ。
English: It means, "'Ow are you?"

Japanese: はあ。‥‥元気ですけど。
English: Oh. Um... I'm fine, thanks.

Japanese: そう。‥‥よかった。
English: Bien. I am 'appy to 'ear it.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、 ハズしたらしいな‥‥）
English: (Doesn't sound like I'm doing so fine with this Psyche-Lock though...)

Japanese: マコちゃんも、きっと 魔がさしちゃったのね‥‥
English: Poor Maggey, she was tempted by la evil, non?

Japanese: （ちがう！　それなら、本土坊さん に、かくす理由がない！）
English: (No! If it was really Maggey, then you'd have no reason to hide the facts from me!)

Japanese: 本土坊さん。‥‥あなたには、 その可能性があるかもしれません。
English: Mr. Armstrong! I believe there is a very high probability that it was you.

Japanese: きゃあああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: （キヌを裂くような オトコのヒメイ‥‥）
English: (Wow! That is one piercing scream, even for a man like him...)

Japanese: ぷ‥‥プルコワ？ なぜ！　ナニを証拠に‥‥！
English: Mais pourquoi moi!? Why!? You 'ave no evidence!

Japanese: ワタシ、仮面マスクじゃないのよ！ ヒトのもの、盗むなんてコト‥‥
English: I am not Mask☆DeMasque! I'm not z'e kind of person who steals z'e property of others.

Japanese: もうしわけありませんが‥‥ 証拠があるんです。
English: Sorry to disappoint you, Mr. Armstrong, but I have evidence to the contrary.

Japanese: あなたは、ヒトのものを 盗んだことがある‥‥！
English: I present to you, proof that you have stolen from others in the past!

Japanese: ‥‥そうね、カモにしましょう。 テリンヌがいいかしら‥‥
English: Oui. Per'aps duck would be nice. A terrine, maybe?

Japanese: はい？
English: Sorry?

Japanese: あ。あしたのランチのコト。 カモのテリンヌ。どうかしら？
English: I was just thinking about my lunch menu. Terrine de canard. What do you think?

Japanese: い、いいんじゃないですか。
English: Th-That sounds lovely...

Japanese: オ・ルヴォア。じゃあワタシ、 買いだしに行ってくるから。
English: Alors. Au revoir, monsieur. I must go to buy la ingredients.

Japanese: （‥‥ちがったか‥‥）
English: (I guess my evidence is a bit of a lame duck...)

Japanese: あらあ‥‥俳句、かしら。 ワタシ、詩にはうるさいのよ。
English: What is z'is!? A poem? Oh, monsieur, you know me so well! I adore poems!

Japanese: 読んでみてください。 ‥‥ゆたかな感情をこめて。
English: Please, read it. And put some feeling into it.

Japanese: “板前はか‥‥っぱらいな‥‥ り常習犯”‥‥
English: "Convicted before, a wicked man... or woman, repeat offender"...

Japanese: 本土坊さん。 あまり言いたくないですけど‥‥
English: I'm sorry to have to bring it up, Mr. Armstrong...

Japanese: お客さんのものを盗んで、 タイホされたことがある、とか‥‥
English: ...but you have been arrested for stealing from your customers before, haven't you?

Japanese: モン・デュッ！
English: Mon dieu!

Japanese: ゆ‥‥ユイ・マンソンジュ！ ウソっぱちよ、そんなの！
English: Le mensonge! You are z'e liar!
You deny it?

Japanese: 言いがかりもイイカゲンに シルヴ・プレるかしら！
English: Do not make la false accusations, s'il vous plaît!

Japanese: そんな証拠はあるの？
English: So do you 'ave any proof?

Japanese: ワタシが‥‥お客さんのモノを 盗んだっていう、決定的な証拠よ！
English: I want to see la incontestable proof that I 'ave ever stolen from one of my customers!

Japanese: おおお‥‥ろおお‥‥かああ‥‥ もおお‥‥のおお‥‥あなた‥‥
English: ♪Im~bécile, im~bécile, U ar an im~bécile...♪

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Wh-What are you singing?

Japanese: ワタシの大好きなシャンソン。 『おろかもの』っていうの。
English: Just one of my favorite chansons. It is called, "Imbécile". It is French.

Japanese: さ。ごいっしょに。
English: Come, monsieur. Sing together w'iz me.

Japanese: おおお‥‥ろおお‥‥かああ‥‥ もおお‥‥のおお‥‥あなた‥‥
English: ♪Im~bécile, im~bécile, U ar an im~bécile...♪

Japanese: （本土坊さんが、ジッサイに 盗んだものがあれば‥‥）
English: (Armstrong's definitely stolen from his customers before...)

Japanese: （決定的な証拠に なるんだけどな‥‥）
English: (I just need to find the right evidence to prove it...)

Japanese: ザンネンですが‥‥まだ、クセは なおってないみたいですね。
English: It seems old habits die hard, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Wh-What is z'at?

Japanese: これは、ぼくの勾玉です。
English: This is my Magatama.

Japanese: ‥‥キッチンで見つけました。
English: And I found it in your kitchen.

Japanese: いやあああああああッ！
English: Nooooon!

Japanese: （なやましげな オトコのヒメイ‥‥）
English: (Wow, that scream just about broke some windows...)

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに‥‥
English: Oui... oui...

Japanese: ワタシ、キレイな アクセサリーや小物が大好き。
English: I-I 'ave a weakness for la little trinkets and la figurines.

Japanese: つい手が出ちゃうコトがあるの。 自分でも止められなくて‥‥。
English: My 'and, it just slips out. I cannot stop it.

Japanese: 客のハンカチとか、手袋なんかを シッケイしたらしいですね。
English: You've stolen handkerchiefs, gloves, and other things from your customers, right?

Japanese: そ、そうなの！
English: Oui! It is la truth!

Japanese: 気の小さい、子リスの ような女の子なのよ、ワタシは！
English: I am just a timid little girl inside, monsieur. A timid little girl!

Japanese: 盗まれたのは、５０００万円。 ‥‥ものすごい金額じゃない！
English: Besides, z'is time, it was not la small trinket, oui? It was 500,000 dollars!

Japanese: ワタシには、必要ないモノ！ 盗むはずがないでしょ！
English: Mais, non! Why would I steal it? I 'ave no need for such money.

Japanese: ‥‥そうでしょうか‥‥
English: Really now?

Japanese: クワ？　なんですって‥‥？
English: Oh, monsieur. What is it?

Japanese: あなたは‥‥大きなモンダイを 抱えていた。
English: Isn't it true that you're in some pretty serious trouble?

Japanese: ‥‥大金が必要だったんじゃ ないんですか？
English: And that you are in desperate need of a large amount of cash?

Japanese: いかがですか、本土坊さん！
English: Well, Mr. Armstrong!?

Japanese: 大きなモンダイを抱えているのは、 アナタのほうみたい。
English: Mais, monsieur. It is not I who is in trouble. It is you, non?
Huh?

Japanese: ケスチョン。
English: Une petite question...

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Wh-What?

Japanese: “モンダイ”というイミよ。 ハイ、ごいっしょに！
English: It means, "a little question". Together now, w'iz me!

Japanese: ケスチョン。
English: Une petite question.

Japanese: ケスチョン。
English: Oon put it keschon.

Japanese: ボン。ボン。‥‥よくできました。
English: Bravo, monsieur! Oh, you are a dream! Excellent!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ダメか）
English: (We're definitely not on the same page here... let alone book...)

Japanese: ‥‥いいかしら？
English: Is z'at all...?

Japanese: ‥‥このレストラン、 かなり苦しいらしいですね。
English: This restaurant is deep in the red, isn't it?
Ah!

Japanese: なんでも、借金をしているとか。 ‥‥５０００万円。
English: You have a loan... to the tune of half a million dollars!

Japanese: 宝くじが手に入れば‥‥ 借金はなくなります。
English: That lottery ticket would have wiped out your debts.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Gnnngh...

Japanese: どうですか！　本土坊さん！
English: Well, Mr. Armstrong!? What do you have to say for yourself now!?
Ah...

Japanese: ああああれええぇぇぇぇぇぇぇっ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 吐麗美庵　キッチン
English: January 6 Trés Bien Kitchen

Japanese: はい。 ここが吐麗美庵のキッチンでーす。
English: And here it is! The famous Trés Bien kitchen!

Japanese: あたしも初めて入るんだけど。
English: It's my first time in here too, actually.

Japanese: やっぱり、ミョーなフンイキだな。
English: There is a weird atmosphere in here, that's for sure.

Japanese: 店長さん、すぐ帰ってくるから、 ぱぱっと調べちゃおうよ！
English: Mr. Armstrong will be back soon, so we'd better search quickly! Chop, chop!

Japanese: おっきなカガミがあるよ。
English: Now THIS is one large mirror!

Japanese: 店長さん、ここで おめかしするんだねー。
English: I bet this is where he makes himself look pretty.

Japanese: 机の上に、手作りの本があるな。 《クラリス・ホンドボーの寝室》。
English: There's a book on the dresser. "Claurice Armstrong's Bedtime Literature".

Japanese: いかがわしいタイトルだね。
English: Not exactly Pulitzer Prize material, is it?

Japanese: これは‥‥本土坊さんの 詩集みたいだな。
English: It looks like a collection of poems he's written.

Japanese: 詩‥‥？　いいねえ。 ちょっと読んでみてよ！
English: Poems? Cool! Read one out!

Japanese: ゆたかな感情をこめて おねがいね。
English: And say it in your best French accent... with itensity! OK?

Japanese: えーと‥‥じゃあ、これ。 タイトルは“ぷらんたん”。
English: OK, um... Here's one. Ahem! It's called, "Printemps".

Japanese: 『‥‥映画のような２人は 今まで気がつかなかったコーヒー
English: "Zee two of zem, like actors from a film. Zee coffee still undrunk.

Japanese: 早すぎるささやきは悲しみの日曜日 意味のない朝は心地よいカクテル
English: Sweet nothings over too soon, on zat sad Sunday morning. Zee foolish cocktail so delicious.

Japanese: あなたが紅茶を飲みほすのなら 私、ウソをついちゃう‥‥きっと』
English: Take zee last sip of your tea and I know what I will do. I must lie to you. I must."

Japanese: ‥‥え？　なに？　おわり？
English: Huh? That's it!?

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まあ、 こんなもんだよ。詩は。
English: Yup. That's a poem for you.

Japanese: この、宝のハコみたいなのは なんだろう。
English: What's this? It looks like a treasure chest or something.

Japanese: うわ、すごい！ 小さなビンがギッシリ。
English: Wow! Look at all these little bottles!

Japanese: アロマテラピーのオイルか。 床の上にまで、あふれてるぞ‥‥
English: Oh! They're aromatherapy oils! He's got so many, they're overflowing onto the floor...

Japanese: １００本はあるよ！ おんなじ小ビンが。
English: Let's see... 1, 2, 3... ...98, 99, 100! They're all the same, too.

Japanese: ‥‥あれ？
English: Hey, wait a minute...

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: What is it?

Japanese: ‥‥このビンだけ、 なんかちがうよ。
English: There's one bottle that's different from all the others.

Japanese: そうだね‥‥。 ラベルもないし。
English: Well what do you know. And it doesn't have a label either.

Japanese: それに‥‥ニオイもしないよ。
English: And... *sniff* It doesn't smell.

Japanese: （‥‥中に液体が入っている。 なんだろう‥‥？）
English: (So what's that liquid inside then, I wonder?)

Japanese: ね。ね。借りておこうか。
English: Hey, Nick, we should borrow this.

Japanese: これだけビンがあったら いいでしょ。１本ぐらい。
English: I mean, look how many bottles he's got. He won't miss one, will he?

Japanese: 証拠品《小ビン》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Small Bottle added to the Court Record.

Japanese: へえ。ちょっと本格的だね。 聞いたことない調味料ばっかり。
English: Now I know I'm in a French restaurant. I've never heard of most of these seasonings.

Japanese: “オイスター・ソース”とか。 なるほどくん、知ってる？
English: Hey, Nick! This container has... oyster sauce? What's that?

Japanese: （それって、中華料理の 調味料じゃなかったっけ）
English: (Isn't that used in Chinese food...?)

Japanese: 包丁もぎらぎらに 研いであるしねー。
English: And look at these knives. They look really sharp!

Japanese: そんな包丁で、みそ汁の わかめ、切ってみたいな。
English: I'd like to see how one of those slices through a cheesecake.

Japanese: せっかくだし、もっと刃ごたえが あるモノを切ろうよ。
English: A cheesecake? You don't exactly need a sharp knife for one of those, Nick.

Japanese: お。いいニオイ。 おナベでグツグツいってるのは‥‥
English: Mmm. That smells good. Something's bubbling away nicely in that pot.

Japanese: オマールえびとアワビのフリカッセ バルサミコ酢風味だね。
English: It must be the lobster and abalone fricassée with a balsamic vinaigrette.

Japanese: さっきのランチじゃないか‥‥
English: Isn't that what I just ate for lunch?

Japanese: それしか知らないんだもん。 フランス料理の名前。
English: Maybe... What you ate is the only French dish I know the name of.

Japanese: ‥‥このレースのカーテンは なんなんだろう。
English: What are these lace curtains for?

Japanese: でも、なんかこう、 いいムードだねえ。
English: I don't know. But they give the place a real homey feel, don't they?

Japanese: こんなところに ぶら下がれるんなら、
English: Hmm... Lace curtains...

Japanese: あたし、ナベになっても いいかも。
English: You know, if I was a cooking pot, I'd be perfectly happy to sit on a shelf under those.

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくとしては なんと答えればいいんだ‥‥？）
English: (How do you respond to something like that!?)

Japanese: へえ。ちょっと本格的だね。 聞いたことない調味料ばっかり。
English: Now I know I'm in a French restaurant. I've never heard of most of these seasonings.

Japanese: “オイスター・ソース”とか。 なるほどくん、知ってる？
English: Hey, Nick! This container has... oyster sauce? What's that?

Japanese: （それって、中華料理の 調味料じゃなかったっけ）
English: (Isn't that used in Chinese food...?)

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！　こ、これ‥‥ テーブルの上に‥‥
English: Ack! Look! Right there on the counter!

Japanese: あああッ！　あたしの勾玉だ！ どうして、こんなところに！
English: My Magatama! What's it doing here!?

Japanese: （どういうコトだ‥‥？）
English: (What indeed...)

Japanese: 《勾玉》をポケットにしまった。
English: Magatama put into pocket.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ビタミン広場
English: January 6 Vitamin Square

Japanese: ここがビタミン広場かあ。
English: So this is Vitamin Square.

Japanese: うーん‥‥たしかに、なぜか “ビタミン！”って感じだな。
English: Yeah. I see where they get the name from now. The fruits scream "VITAMINS!" at you.

Japanese: あ。なるほどくん！
English: Hey, Nick!

Japanese: あの人じゃないかな。
English: That's the guy, right?

Japanese: 店長さんが言ってた “オジさん”って。
English: Isn't that the old man Mr. Armstrong was talking about?

Japanese: あ、あの ガンコそうなオヤジか‥‥
English: That grouchy-looking grandpa?

Japanese: ハトにエサ、やってるよ。
English: He's throwing seeds out for the pigeons.

Japanese: エサをやってる、というより‥‥ たたきつけてるように見えるな。
English: Maya, he's not throwing seeds for them, he's throwing seeds AT them!

Japanese: （う‥‥。強いキョゼツの オーラを感じる‥‥）
English: (Ugh... My grumpiness-threat level has just been raised to red...)

Japanese: フルーティーだよね。 なんていうか。
English: This place is so fruity!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、いいんじゃないかな。 ぼくもリンゴは大好きだし。
English: That's not a bad thing. Besides, I love apples. They're among my favorites.

Japanese: あ！　じゃあ、あの すべり台、もってこいじゃない！
English: Then that apple slide is perfect for you!

Japanese: ‥‥なにがどう “もってこい”なんだよ。
English: And what is so "perfect" about it?

Japanese: はいはい、なるほどくん。 すべってすべって。
English: Oh come on. Don't be a stick in the mud! Slide down it a few times. Go on! Woo!

Japanese: や、やだよ。
English: No way!

Japanese: すべったら、かならず砂まみれに なるシステムだぞ、あれ。
English: I'd get covered in sand if I slid down that slide. Anyone can see that.

Japanese: ううん、たしかに。 それがなきゃ、あたしもねえ‥‥
English: Yeah, you're right. Otherwise, I'd give it a try, too.

Japanese: ハトだ。
English: Hey, look. Pigeons!

Japanese: たくさんいるね。
English: Yeah. And heaps of them, too.

Japanese: ね。知ってる？　ハトって、 平和のシンボルなんだよ！
English: Did you know that pigeons are a symbol of peace?

Japanese: そりゃ、白いハトだけだろ。
English: That's a dove, not a pigeon.

Japanese: かわいそうだね、この子たち。
English: Poor things.

Japanese: 色が白くないだけなのに‥‥ 平和になりそこねちゃって。
English: Just because they're gray means they can't be symbols of peace and harmony?

Japanese: な、何もそこまで しんみりしなくても‥‥
English: You're over-thinking this one by just a smidge, Maya.

Japanese: いやあ、砂場には 燃えたねー、あたしも。
English: I used to love sandboxes like you wouldn't believe!

Japanese: 燃えた？　砂場遊びに？
English: Really? You?

Japanese: ホラ。じしゃくを使って 砂鉄あつめ。
English: Sure. Finding iron filings in the sand with a magnet was my favorite thing to do.

Japanese: サテツ‥‥地味だなあ。
English: Iron filings? Wow... That's too exciting for words.

Japanese: 『砂場じゅうの砂鉄を 集めきる！』って決意して。
English: It was my ambition to collect every single shred of iron in the sandbox.

Japanese: あたしも若かったな。 あのころは。
English: I was such a kid back then!

Japanese: ‥‥で、集めきったの？
English: So, did you manage to get all the iron?

Japanese: ううん。 もう少しだったんだけどねえ。
English: No, I never did. I think I came close, though.

Japanese: あ。そういえば、まだ とってあるよ！　そのときの砂鉄。
English: Come to think of it, I still have all the iron filings I found way back when.

Japanese: いる？
English: You want them?

Japanese: いらない。
English: No.

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。雑誌が１さつ、 ほっぽり出してあるよ。
English: Ooh, there's a magazine here.

Japanese: アルバイトの情報誌か。 《シゴト誌》っていうタイトルだ。
English: It's a magazine full of job listings.

Japanese: はっ！　ワシが捨てたモノを 拾うとは、イヤしいオトコだな！
English: You disgusting rogue, picking up something someone else threw away!

Japanese: “捨てた”って‥‥、オジさん、 仕事、探してるんですか？
English: "Threw away"? Did you throw this away? Are you looking for a job?

Japanese: ムグ。 ‥‥キサマにはカンケイないワ！
English: Kah! That's none of your business!

Japanese: とりあえず、もらっておくか。 ‥‥シゴト誌。
English: Sorry! I guess I'll just take the magazine with me then.

Japanese: 他人の手にわたると思うと もったいなくなってきた。返せ！
English: I don't want anyone else having it! Give it back!

Japanese: （そう言われると、 返したくなくなるよな‥‥）
English: (Too bad. Now that you want it so bad, I don't want to give it up!)

Japanese: 《シゴト誌》をやや強引に拾った。
English: Job Listings added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ワシのだ、それ！
English: Hey! That's mine!

Japanese: あのさ。このみかん見てたら 思い出したんだけど。
English: Looking at this orange reminds me...

Japanese: ‥‥なに？
English: Of what?

Japanese: やっぱり、お正月といえば、 おもち食べながらみかん食べて、
English: That you're supposed to eat a lot of them to ward off colds in the winter.

Japanese: おせちをつつきながらテレビ見て、 さらにトランプでババ抜きだよね。
English: You can't have fun during the holidays if you're sick in bed, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥それはもういいよ。
English: You don't have to tell me twice.

Japanese: あの‥‥すみません。
English: Um... Excuse me.

Japanese: ちょっといいですか？
English: Would you mind if I had a word with you?

Japanese: よくないッ！
English: Yes!

Japanese: お話が聞きたいんですけど‥‥
English: I just want to ask you a couple of questions...

Japanese: 見てわからんか！ いそがしいんだワシは！
English: What's the matter with you!? Can't you see I'm busy?

Japanese: ハトどもめッ！ ワシのマメが食えんと言うかッ！
English: So you don't like my seeds, eh, pigeons!?

Japanese: （ハトにマメをたたきつけてる だけじゃないか‥‥）
English: (He's really chucking those seeds at them! That's got to hurt!)

Japanese: 食らえ！　この！
English: Go on! Eat this!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (*sigh*)

Japanese: すみません。 マコちゃんのことですけど。
English: Excuse me, sir. Can I ask you about Maggey Byrde?

Japanese: ‥‥マコチャン？
English: I don't know any Maggey Byrde!

Japanese: はい！ 吐麗美庵の、ウエイトレスさん。
English: Yes, you do. Maggey. The waitress at Trés Bien.

Japanese: フン！　なげかわしいわ！
English: Kah! It's a disgrace, I tell you. An utter disgrace!

Japanese: な、なげかわしい？
English: A disgrace?

Japanese: うら若い婦女子が、あんな はしたないカッコウで！
English: An innocent young girl revealing herself like that!

Japanese: は、“はしたない”って‥‥ あの制服のコトですか？
English: Revealing!? You mean her uniform?

Japanese: ま、まったく、最近の婦女子は “恥ぢらい”というモノを知らん！
English: The youth of today! They don't have any shame! No shame, I tell you! Not one ounce!

Japanese: ああ、ヤマトナデシコは 枯れはててしまったワイ！
English: Whatever happened to the old bushido values of Japan, like honor and modesty?

Japanese: ‥‥あたし、和服着てますよ？
English: What about me? I'm not wearing anything revealing.

Japanese: アンタは、しとやかさが足りん！
English: You!? Your problem is you lack any sense of grace!

Japanese: キメつけられた‥‥。
English: Talk about hitting a girl where it hurts...

Japanese: すみません。 マコちゃんのことですけど。
English: Excuse me, sir. Can I ask you about Maggey Byrde?

Japanese: ‥‥マコチャン？
English: I don't know any Maggey Byrde!

Japanese: ほら。この子ですよ。 吐麗美庵のウエイトレス。
English: Yes, you do. You know, the waitress at Trés Bien.
Hmph!

Japanese: わ、ワシはカンシンせんな。 こ、こんな‥‥その。
English: If you ask me, it's a disgrace! Th-That's what it is!

Japanese: うら若い婦女子が、こんな はしたないカッコウで‥‥
English: An innocent young girl revealing herself like that!

Japanese: キライなんですか。 こういうのは。
English: So, you don't like that kind of thing, then?

Japanese: な、なんじゃそれは！ どーゆうイミじゃそれは！
English: Wh-What are you trying to say about me, boy!? Explain yourself!

Japanese: え！　い、いや、べつに 深いイミは‥‥
English: Um, nothing! I'm not saying anything about you...

Japanese: どーせ、キサマのギザギザの アタマん中じゃァ、
English: I know your kind! You young ones with your spiky hair and your fancy suits!

Japanese: フラチなコトしか 考えとらんのだろ！
English: That head of yours is over- flowing with filthy ideas!

Japanese: （ドナりつけられた‥‥）
English: (...I know someone who needs to learn some stress management.)

Japanese: 帰れ、ワカゾウがッ！
English: Get out of here, you young brat!

Japanese: 吐麗美庵、 よく行くんですよね？
English: Do you go to Trés Bien a lot?

Japanese: ふん！　なんだあの店は！
English: Hmph! That miserable excuse for a restaurant?

Japanese: そばもさしみも天ぷらも、 ひややっこすらない！
English: That garbage they serve in there's not food. Where's the sushi? The tempura? The rice!?

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃ、 フランス料理の店ですからね。
English: Trés Bien is a French restaurant, sir...

Japanese: なあアンタ。ここはどこだ？ ロンドンか？　ニッポンだろうが！
English: Where do you think we are, boy? In Paris!? I want real food, not those snooty snacks!

Japanese: それに、あの、こっぱずかしい 婦女子どもめら。
English: And what about those shameless girls!?

Japanese: ウエイトレスですか？
English: You mean the waitresses?

Japanese: ふとももに‥‥、むむ。 その、なんだ。
English: You can see all the way up to their... their... Yes! The waitresses!

Japanese: まるだしじゃないかッ！ どうなんだコラッ！
English: They're practically naked! It's a disgrace, isn't it? Well, isn't it!?

Japanese: す、すみませんッ！
English: Listen, it's not my restaurant.

Japanese: なんなんだ、あの店はァッ！
English: Kah! It's a miserable excuse for a restaurant, that place! Miserable!

Japanese: （このオジさん、たしか “常連”だったよな‥‥）
English: (He certainly knows the place. He must be a regular!)

Japanese: （そんなにキライなら、 行かなきゃいいのに‥‥）
English: (But if he hates it so much, why does he keep going?)

Japanese: オジさん‥‥あの店の常連さん なんですよね？
English: Are you a regular at that restaurant, sir?

Japanese: そんなにキライなら、 行かなきゃいいじゃないですか。
English: It's just, if you dislike it so much, why would you keep going there?

Japanese: ‥‥オジさん？
English: Sir?

Japanese: 食らえッ！　ハトども！ 食らわんと言うかッ！
English: There you are, you filthy pigeons! You want food? Ha-ha! Take that!

Japanese: オジさん‥‥ナニか かくしてるのかな？
English: He must be hiding something, right?

Japanese: でも、それならば サイコ・ロックが見えるはず‥‥
English: If he is, I should be able to see a Psyche-Lock...
Oh, wait...

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥今、 勾玉がないんだっけ‥‥）
English: I don't exactly have the Magatama right now, huh...

Japanese: あれ、貸してるだけだからね。
English: Remember, Nick. That Magatama is only on loan.

Japanese: ちゃんと見つけるんだよ、 なるほどくん！
English: You'd better find it or else!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (If Pearls ever gets wind of this, I'm going to be in a world of pain...)

Japanese: あの‥‥ちょっと、お話を うかがいたいんですけど‥‥
English: Um, excuse me, sir. Could I just ask you about this...
Hmm, ha-hmm, hmm...

Japanese: あの、すみません。
English: Sir?

Japanese: 食らえッ！　このハトめらッ！
English: Here you go, boy! How does some pigeon feed sound to you!?

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥ゼンゼン 相手にしてくれない‥‥）
English: (This wasn't exactly what I was hoping to get out of this guy...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ビタミン広場
English: January 6 Vitamin Square

Japanese: あれ‥‥あのオジさん、 いなくなっちまったぞ。
English: Hmm, the old guy's not here anymore.

Japanese: （こまったな‥‥まだ、 聞くべきことがあったのに）
English: (Drat. And I still have some unanswered questions for him.)

Japanese: スクーターだ。わざわざ 公園のドまん中に置いておくとは。
English: A scooter, huh? Who'd leave it right in the middle of the park like this?

Japanese: タイヤのカバーやら、ライトやら、 ハデにブッ壊れてるな‥‥
English: The wheel guard and the light are busted...

Japanese: 事故でもあったのかな。 ‥‥まあ、ボロっちいコト‥‥
English: I guess it must have been in an accident. It's totally wrecked.

Japanese: コォラァアアアアアアアアアアアア アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: Gwoaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

Japanese: ッ！
English: aaaaaaaar!

Japanese: ヒトの愛車に ナニしとるんやワレェェッ！
English: Hey! Whad'youse think youse doin' wit my bike!?

Japanese: い、いえベツに、ぼくは
English: N-No! I was just...

Japanese: うおわあああああああああああッ！
English: Gwoaaaaaaaaaaaar!

Japanese: ワイのカワイイ新車が、 ボコボコにされとるやんけェェッ！
English: Youse been messin' with my new ride? Is that what youse been doin'!?

Japanese: し‥‥新車って‥‥ それ、かなり古いタイプの
English: N-New ride? Isn't that kind of an old model?

Japanese: キサマやなッ！
English: Gwoar! Youse gonna pay for dis!

Japanese: ちち、ちがいますよ！ ぼくは、通りすがりの
English: It-It wasn't me! I was just passing by.

Japanese: それに、なんや！　サドルが クソまみれになっとるやんけェッ！
English: Ey! Den who'z de one tat covered my saddle in crap!? Huh?

Japanese: キサマやなッ！
English: Gwoar! Youse gonna pay, you' catch my drift!?

Japanese: い、いやいや！ ぼく、ハトじゃありませんから
English: No! Wait a sec! I'm not a pigeon! So I couldn't have done it!

Japanese: ‥‥エエ度胸やなァ、ニイちゃん。
English: A wise guy, eh?

Japanese: ボテくりこかしまわしてさらにコレ シバきたおしたろかいワレェェッ！
English: I atta beat you so hard, it'll feel like youse were smoochin' the express train!
(Uh-oh!)

Japanese: とにかく、タダじゃ すませへんでェ。
English: Youse better watch your back! This ain't over!

Japanese: 弁護士の１ダースも 用意しときィィッ！
English: I'm gonna round up a group of lawyers and den youse gonna pay!

Japanese: あの‥‥いちおう、 弁護士ですけど‥‥ぼく。
English: Um... Actually... I'm a lawyer myself...

Japanese: なんやて‥‥？
English: Whad'youse say?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一です。 ‥‥よろしく
English: I'm Phoenix Wright, attorney at law.

Japanese: ぐわああああああっはっはっはっ はっはっはっはっはっはっはァァ！
English: Gwoaaaaaaaah ha ha ha hah! Ha ha ha ha hah!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一ィィィ？ アンタがかァ？
English: Phoenix Wright? Youse sayin' YOU' Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥ まあ、そうですけど。
English: Um, yeah... I am.

Japanese: バカも休み休み言いやァ、 兄ちゃんよ‥‥。
English: So you' a wiseguy too, huh?

Japanese: 弁護士の成歩堂　龍一ゆうたら‥‥ この、ワイのコトやああぁぁぁッ！
English: Cause I'M Phoenix Wright. The one and only!

Japanese: ええええええええええええええッ！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: オラ！　ジャマやああッ！
English: Outta my way! I gotta cruise.

Japanese: （い‥‥行っちまった‥‥）
English: (He-He's gone...)

Japanese: ま‥‥まさか、今のヤツが‥‥ ぼくのニセモノ‥‥？
English: Surely that guy wasn't my phony... was he...?

Japanese: ゼンゼン似てないじゃないか！
English: He wasn't anything like me!!

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、スクーターの コト、メモしておくか‥‥）
English: (Guess I better make a note of the scooter...)

Japanese: 《スクーター》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Scooter added to the Court Record.

Japanese: かァ！　まったく、情けないワ！
English: Kah! Pathetic!

Japanese: お、オジさん‥‥
English: Oh, it's you.

Japanese: あんなニイちゃんにドナられた ぐらいで、
English: A few threats from a little brat like that...

Japanese: ハトに食われたマメみたいな カオしおってからに！
English: ...and you look like a pigeon that's got seeds in its eyes.

Japanese: ‥‥オジさんは、今まで どこにいたんですか‥‥？
English: Have you been here the whole time then?

Japanese: あのイチゴの中で 考えゴトじゃい！
English: I was in that strawberry. I had some thinking to do.

Japanese: （ヒナンしてたんじゃないか！）
English: (More like you had some cowering to do!)

Japanese: 最近、見かけなくなったよな、 こういう公園。
English: You don't see many parks like this anymore.

Japanese: これで子どもたちが遊んでいれば カンペキなんだけど‥‥
English: What a great place for kids to play.

Japanese: アヤしいジイさんが、マメを まき散らしているだけだからなあ。
English: But that old man's the only one here, chucking his seeds at the pigeons.

Japanese: ハトがムチュウになって マメをついばんでいる。
English: The pigeons are so busy pecking at the seeds, they don't notice anything else.

Japanese: あくまでも無心に、マメに 向かってゆくハトを見ていると‥‥
English: Seeing those birds eat with such intense focus...

Japanese: ムチュウになってラーメンすすって いる真宵ちゃんを思い出すなあ。
English: ...reminds me of Maya when she's scarfing down a burger.

Japanese: 今にして思えば‥‥
English: When I think back to when I was young...

Japanese: 何が楽しかったんだろ、 砂場遊びって。
English: ...I wonder what I found so great about playing in a sandbox.

Japanese: 今度、夜中にコッソリ、 遊びに来てみようかな‥‥
English: Maybe I'll come back here in the middle of the night one of these days to relive it.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事に タイホされちまうか。
English: On second thought, being arrested by Gumshoe would be too embarrassing to bear.

Japanese: そういえば、さっき そこで雑誌を拾ったっけ。
English: This is where I found that job listings magazine before.

Japanese: スーツを着てこんなもの 抱えて歩くの、みっともないな。
English: A guy like me carrying around a magazine like that kind of gives the wrong impression.

Japanese: 用がすんだら、 あのオジさんに返そうかな。
English: I guess I better give it back to that old man once this investigation's over.

Japanese: オジさん‥‥あの店の常連さん なんですよね？
English: Are you a regular at that restaurant, sir?

Japanese: そんなにキライなら、 行かなきゃいいじゃないですか。
English: It's just that if you dislike it so much, why would you keep going there?

Japanese: ‥‥オジさん？
English: Sir?

Japanese: 食らえッ！　ハトども！ 食らわんと言うかッ！
English: There you are, you filthy pigeons! You want food? Ha-ha! Take that!

Japanese: （や‥‥やっぱり‥‥）
English: (I knew it...)

Japanese: （このジイさん、何か ヒミツがあるのか‥‥？）
English: (This old guy has got something to hide. But what could it be?)

Japanese: オジさん。どうして キライな店の常連なんですか？
English: Why are you a regular at a restaurant that you clearly hate, sir?

Japanese: ‥‥食らえッ！　この、ハトがッ！
English: Take that, pigeons! See how you like these seeds!

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ！　ロックを 解除するしかなさそうだな‥‥）
English: (It's no good. I'm gonna have to break his Psyche-Lock.)

Japanese: （それにしても‥‥ かくすようなコトなのか？）
English: (I just can't figure out what he would be hiding.)

Japanese: （‥‥レストランに行く理由 なんて‥‥）
English: (I mean, I'm only asking him why he goes to a particular restaurant.)

Japanese: あの‥‥。おジイさん‥‥？
English: Um... Sir...

Japanese: ああそうさ！　どうせワシは 若い女給さんが目当てさ！
English: Yes, it's true! I was there for the young girl.

Japanese: うすぎたない、イロにおぼれた、 フケツでおぞましい初老の怪物さ！
English: Fine, so I'm a dirty, wicked, sinful old devil!

Japanese: い‥‥いやいや、なにもそんな‥‥
English: No, no... I didn't mean it like that...

Japanese: まずいコーシーも飲みくだすさ！ ９８０円だって払うさ！
English: I even get one of those lousy cups of javaccino every time. For 8 dollars!

Japanese: 若い女給さんのタメならな！ フン！　ケーベツするがいい！
English: All because of the serving girl! Punish me! Lock me up!

Japanese: だから、そういうつもりじゃあ‥‥
English: Really, that's not what I'm here for.

Japanese: キサマだって。あと２０年もすりゃ イヤでもわかるのだ！
English: You'll be the same! Another 20 years and you'll understand what it's like!

Japanese: ワシのこの、せつないキモチがッ！
English: You'll know how painful it is to be an old man like me!

Japanese: い、いや。だから、おジイさん‥‥
English: No, really. Listen, sir...

Japanese: そもそも、キサマ！ おジイさんって言うな！
English: Stop calling me that!

Japanese: 名前ぐらいある！　五十嵐　将兵 （いがらししょうへい）だッ！
English: I have a name you know, boy! So show some respect, hmm!? I'm Victor Kudo.

Japanese: す、すみません。 イガラシさん‥‥？
English: S-Sorry... Mr. Kudo.

Japanese: 若いからってイバるな！ もう、ナニもしゃべらん！
English: You young ones think you know it all, don't you? Well, I'm not saying another word!

Japanese: 教えてやらんからねーだ！
English: I won't tell you anything more!

Japanese: （‥‥このオジさんは 事件当時、あの店にいた）
English: (This guy was in the restaurant at the time of the incident.)

Japanese: （なんとしても、証言を 聞く必要があるんだ‥‥！）
English: (Which means I have to hear his testimony, one way or another!)

Japanese: ‥‥ぷいっ！
English: Hmph!

Japanese: （せっかくサイコ・ロックを 解除したってのに‥‥）
English: (I don't believe this... I even broke his Psyche-Lock and everything...)

Japanese: （なんとかして、キゲンを なおしてもらわないと‥‥！）
English: (I guess I'll have to try to get him when he's in a better mood.)

Japanese: 五十嵐さん！　おねがいします。 重要なコトなんですよ！
English: Mr. Kudo! Please! This is really important.

Japanese: ぽっぽっぽお！　ハトぽっぽお ‥‥‥‥とぉ！
English: Hmm, ha-hmm, hmm... Hmm, pi-pi-pigeon, hmm... Kah!

Japanese: （ダメだ！　致命的に ヘソを曲げてしまった‥‥！）
English: (Ack. He's really giving me the cold shoulder now.)

Japanese: （このオジさんは、重要な目撃者。 ゼッタイに話を聞く必要がある）
English: (But this guy's an important witness. I can't afford to lose his testimony.)

Japanese: （‥‥キゲンをなおしてもらう 方法が、何かあるはずだ‥‥！）
English: (There must be a way to soften him up...)

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)

Japanese: ～常連さん～
English: -- Trés Bien Regular --

Japanese: 聞かせてもらいましょうか。
English: It's time you told me the truth.

Japanese: なぜ、大キライなレストランに 常連として通っているのか。
English: Why are you a regular at a restaurant that you dislike so much?

Japanese: 聞くまでもなかろう！
English: Isn't it obvious!?

Japanese: ニンゲンが食い物屋に足をはこぶ 理由はただ１つ。食うタメだ！
English: People only have one reason to go to restaurants. To eat!

Japanese: 食べるため‥‥ ホントにそうなんですか？
English: To eat? Is that the whole truth?

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: あの店の料理を目当てに 通っているとは思えません。
English: I don't think you go to that restaurant for the food at all.

Japanese: な、なんだとこのワカゾウがッ！ ナニを証拠に、そんな‥‥ッ！
English: You insolent brat! How dare you accuse me! What proof have you got!?

Japanese: あの店の料理が 目当てではないという証拠は‥‥！
English: I can tell that not you nor anyone else in the world would go to that place for its food.

Japanese: どうですか！
English: Well?

Japanese: 食らえッ！
English: Take this!

Japanese: うわっ！
English: Argh!

Japanese: キサマのノーミソにくれてやるワ！ たっぷり食えッ！
English: You're a disgrace! An idiot! A clot! A fool! You're only worthy of this pigeon feed!

Japanese: いてててててて！ （どうやら、はずしたらしい‥‥）
English: Ow! (Looks like that evidence wasn't it!)

Japanese: と、とにかく‥‥！
English: L-Listen...

Japanese: 証拠は‥‥ ランチそのものですよ。
English: The proof is in the pudding. Or in this case, the lunch menu.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ニクハチセット！
English: That's the Twin-T set!

Japanese: 吐麗美庵の料理は‥‥ とてもマズくて、そして、高い！
English: The food at Trés Bien is terrible. And expensive.

Japanese: 安いモンだ。
English: You're wrong. It's cheap.

Japanese: は？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ワシはな。いいトコの ボッちゃんなんだよ！
English: I'm rich. I inherited money when I was a boy. Yes, I'm stinking rich!

Japanese: 生まれてこのかた、ハトのマメより 重いものは持ったことがない！
English: I haven't done a jot of work since I was born. Other than feeding the pigeons.

Japanese: （ウソつけ！）
English: (What a load of crock!)

Japanese: 味はともかく、値段が 手ゴロだからな、あの店は。
English: The taste's another story, but the price... It's nothing to me.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥お金があまってるから、 あの店へ‥‥？
English: So, you're saying that you go there because you've got money to burn?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうともさ！
English: Exactly! I have so much cash, I go for a swim in my money vault every day!

Japanese: それはウソですね。
English: Unfortunately, that's a lie.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと！
English: What!?

Japanese: あなたのサイフに、おカネが あまっているハズがない。
English: You don't have money to burn. You're flat broke!

Japanese: ‥‥シッケイなッ！
English: Disrespectful youth!
Uh?

Japanese: この、最高級のマメを かっ食らうがいいッ！
English: Take this! The finest seeds money can buy! Ha-ha!

Japanese: いてててててて！ （どうやら、ちがったらしい‥‥）
English: Ouuuch! (These seeds really hurt when they land in your eyes!)

Japanese: ワシはな！　それはリッチな ごインキョなんだ！
English: I'm rich, I tell you! Never worked in my life! As rich as they come!

Japanese: 何度でも言います。 そんなハズ、ないんですよ。
English: I don't believe you. You're lying to me.

Japanese: これ‥‥、たしか あなたのでしたよね？
English: This is yours, right?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それは‥‥
English: My magazine!

Japanese: リッチなごインキョさんが、 なぜシゴトを探してるんですか！
English: Why would a rich retiree be looking for a job?

Japanese: ぐぐ‥‥し、シゴトォォォォッ！
English: I-I-I was... Argh! So what!?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだよ！　ワシは今、 モーレツにこづかいがほしいんだ！
English: So I was looking for a job! I'm buying a lot at the moment. I need spending money.

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: Oh?

Japanese: あの店で、ランチなんか食わん！ コーシーを飲みに行くんだ！
English: I don't go to that restaurant for food. I just go for the javaccino.

Japanese: コーヒーはいくらなんですか？
English: Yeah... I think you mean a cappuccino. Anyway, how much does a cappuccino cost there?
Eight dollars.

Japanese: （高ェ！）
English: (Those had better be some golden beans!)

Japanese: お？　なんだなんだ！
English: What's your problem?

Japanese: ビンボー人はドロ水でも飲んどけ。 ‥‥こう言うのかアンタは！
English: You think a poor man would be better off drinking dishwater, do you? Is that it!?

Japanese: い、いえ‥‥そういうワケじゃ ないですけど‥‥
English: N-No. I wasn't thinking that.

Japanese: そんなおいしい コーヒーでしたっけ？
English: I was wondering if the coffee there is really that great...
