Japanese: う。‥‥そうでもない。 し、しかし！
English: No, it's not. But...

Japanese: あそこなら‥‥そう！ 毎日、タダで新聞が読めるんだよ！
English: But anyway! Yes! That place has free newspapers to read every day.

Japanese: 新聞を‥‥
English: Newspapers?

Japanese: そうさ！　ウチじゃジャマモノ あつかいされておるからな！
English: Exactly. They don't want me hanging around at home, so I go there.

Japanese: 申しワケないですけど‥‥
English: I'm sorry, sir...

Japanese: あの店で毎日、新聞をタダで 読むことはできません！
English: But there are no free papers to read at Trés Bien!

Japanese: どうですか！
English: Well?

Japanese: ‥‥キサマ、そんなに マメが大好物か‥‥
English: You like seeds, do you, boy?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥？
English: Sorry?

Japanese: お望みどおり、 食らわせてやるワ！
English: If you like them so much, then how about them seeds!?

Japanese: ホラ！　マメだ！　食え！ 明日のためにッ！
English: Now eat up, boy! You heard me!

Japanese: いてててててて！ （どうやら、ダメみたいだ‥‥）
English: Ow! Oooow! (Well, oww! That was no good... Oww!)

Japanese: 思い知ったか、ワカゾウがッ！
English: Well, boy? How do you like them seeds, hmm?

Japanese: これを見てください。
English: Take a look at this.

Japanese: ‥‥なんじゃい。
English: What is it?

Japanese: 吐麗美庵のマガジンラックに あった、新聞です。
English: It's a newspaper I found behind the magazine rack at Trés Bien.

Japanese: ケッ！　それがどうしたよ？
English: So? What of it?

Japanese: 新聞はこれしかありませんでした。 日付は‥‥１ヶ月も前です。
English: This was the only paper there. And it's dated more than one month ago!

Japanese: な‥‥ナニッ！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: いいですか！　あの店では、 新聞は置いてないんですよ！
English: Do you see what I'm getting at here? That restaurant doesn't get newspapers.

Japanese: これはたまたま、 お客が忘れていっただけなんです！
English: This is just one that a customer happened to leave behind!

Japanese: う‥‥うおごおおおッ！
English: Ah... Aaaaaargh!

Japanese: ‥‥オジさん。なぜ そこまでして、かくすんですか？
English: Tell me, why are you so determined to hide the truth?

Japanese: ワシは‥‥ワシはその。 ナニも、かくしてなどおらんワ！
English: I-I'm not hiding anything!

Japanese: （そろそろ、 ラクにしてあげるか‥‥）
English: (I'm going to have to put this guy out of his misery.)

Japanese: ‥‥オジさん。
English: Listen...

Japanese: 吐麗美庵に通っていたのは‥‥ もしかして、このためでは？
English: The real reason why you go so much to Trés Bien is...

Japanese: どうですか？
English: Well?

Japanese: ワーハハハ！ ちがうもんねー！
English: Ah ha ha! You're wrong, you're wrong!

Japanese: ‥‥幼児化してきた。 キケンだな‥‥
English: Uh-oh. He's gone silly.

Japanese: ダレがしゃべるもんかい！ フンだ！
English: You're so stupid! Ha ha ha!

Japanese: （料理でも、コーヒーでもない。 新聞でもない‥‥）
English: (It's not the food, it's not the coffee, and it's not the newspapers...)

Japanese: （残るのは‥‥なんだ？）
English: (What else is there?)

Japanese: な‥‥なな、なんだ、 そのムスメは‥‥
English: Wh-What are you asking me about that girl for?

Japanese: あの店の、 ウエイトレスさんですよ。
English: She was the waitress at Trés Bien.

Japanese: うぐッ‥‥！
English: Ah!

Japanese: おカネのないおジイさんが マズい店で高いコーヒーを飲む‥‥
English: Therefore, the answer to the mystery...

Japanese: ‥‥そのナゾの答えは‥‥
English: ...of why an old man would drink expensive coffee at a terrible restaurant...

Japanese: ウエイトレスさんだったのです！
English: ...is the waitress!

Japanese: ぎゃっ！
English: Argh!

Japanese: わ‥‥ワシは知らん！ そんな婦女子のカオは‥‥
English: B-But I don't recognize that face!

Japanese: カオを見ていない‥‥ そうかもしれませんね。
English: And you are probably telling the truth here.

Japanese: あなたが見ていたのは‥‥
English: Because you weren't looking at the girl's face...

Japanese: ウエイトレスさんの制服だった！
English: But at her outfit!

Japanese: むがッ！
English: *gulp*

Japanese: おジイさん！
English: That's the truth, isn't it?

Japanese: あなたは、ウエイトレスさんの 制服が目当てで、店に通っていた！
English: You became a regular at the restaurant because of the waitress's uniform!

Japanese: そう！　ウエイトレスさんの 制服を、目に焼きつけるためにッ！
English: That uniform is all you can think about, isn't it?

Japanese: ‥‥う‥‥う‥‥ダマって 聞いておれば、さっきから‥‥
English: Uh... uh... I-I can't take it...

Japanese: ウエイトレスさんこそが、 おジイさんにとっての‥‥
English: To you, that waitress was your...

Japanese: うるさいワッ！　たのむから‥‥
English: Enough! Please! No more!

Japanese: ウエイトレスさんウエイトレスさん と連呼しないでくれェェェェッ！
English: Stop saying that word! Stop saying, "waitress"! Stop it, stop it, stop it!

Japanese: あの。オジさん‥‥？
English: Um, sir...

Japanese: フン！　キサマには‥‥
English: Hmph! You again?

Japanese: ふうむ‥‥ほお。なるほど‥‥
English: Hmm... Well, well... I see...

Japanese: なな、なるほどくん‥‥ キョーレツな視線を感じるよ！
English: Uh, Nick. His eyes are burning into me!

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。 うまくいってるみたいだ。
English: It's OK. I think it's going pretty well.
Kah!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: まだまだ、オコサマだの。
English: You're still just a little child!

Japanese: そこのすべり台で おゆうぎでもしとれ！
English: Run along and play on the slide, alright?

Japanese: ‥‥おゆうぎ‥‥
English: P-Play on the slide?

Japanese: （ううん‥‥ もう少しだったのに‥‥）
English: (Argh! We were so close! Just a little more and he would've spilled...)

Japanese: ぽっぽっぽお！　ハトぽっぽお ‥‥‥‥とぉ！
English: Hmm, ha-hmm, hmm... Hmm, pi-pi-pigeon, hmm... Kah!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (How can we crack this guy?)

Japanese: あの‥‥。 ちょっとよろしいかしら‥‥
English: Umm, excuse me please, sir...

Japanese: ウルサイ！　ワシは今、 ハト‥‥に‥‥エサ‥‥を‥‥
English: Quiet! Can't you see I'm feeding... the... pige...

Japanese: ち‥‥ 千尋さん‥‥！
English: Mia!?

Japanese: ‥‥むほッ！
English: Well...

Japanese: もしよろしかったら‥‥
English: If you don't mind, sir...

Japanese: 私とおしゃべり、 していただけないかしら。
English: I'd really love to talk with you.

Japanese: い。い。い。 ‥‥五十嵐　将兵と申す。
English: Yes. Yes, yes. Of course. Certainly. I'm Victor. Victor Kudo.
(Even from beyond the grave... Wow...)

Japanese: 事件のコトですけど‥‥。
English: About the incident...

Japanese: ああ、アレですかな。 客がコーシー飲んで死んだヤツ。
English: You mean, the man who died after drinking the javaccino?

Japanese: （‥‥性格が変わったぞ‥‥）
English: (It's like he's a different person...)

Japanese: ビックリしましたなあ、 さすがのワシも！
English: It was quite a shock! Even for me.

Japanese: なんか、ミョーなカッコウを したワカゾウでしたわい！
English: He was a strange-looking boy.

Japanese: 女給さんが、コーシーを 運んだワケですわ、その男に！
English: The girl took the javaccino over to him, you see.

Japanese: お客さんは‥‥ひとり、 だったのかしら？
English: And... was the customer alone?

Japanese: マチガイない！　あの席には、 ソイツしかいませんでしたぞ！
English: Definitely! He was the only person at the table.

Japanese: で、その客がコーシーを ヒトクチ飲んだら‥‥。
English: Then he took one sip of his javaccino, and...

Japanese: 飲んだら？
English: And?

Japanese: うぎゃーっとこう、 倒れちまったわけだ！
English: And he said something like, "Uaaaargh!" and then collapsed. Dead.

Japanese: まあ‥‥コワい！
English: Oh, how terrifying!

Japanese: おじょうさん、 聞きじょうずですなあ。
English: You're so good at listening, aren't you?

Japanese: オジさん、いくらでも しゃべれそうだワイ。うははは。
English: I'll tell you anything. Whatever you want to know! Hee hee hee.

Japanese: （ウソをついているようすは まったく、ない‥‥）
English: (He certainly seems to be telling the truth now.)

Japanese: （五十嵐さんは、見ていないんだ！ “もうひとりの男”を‥‥）
English: (But it looks like Mr. Kudo didn't see this other man, either.)

Japanese: 吐麗美庵のお料理は、 おクチに合いまして？
English: Do you like the food at Trés Bien?

Japanese: え！　そ、それはもう！
English: Well, of course! I'm really quite a sophisticated man.

Japanese: ワシはその、若いコロ‥‥ 青年実業家でしてなァ！
English: I was a young businessman once, you know.

Japanese: ロンドンでパチンコ屋、 やっとったんですワ！
English: I set up a casino in London!

Japanese: まあ‥‥。
English: Really? How interesting!

Japanese: あそこの料理を食うと、その。 思い出すわけですわ、フランスを！
English: Eating the food at that restaurant really takes me back to my days in France.

Japanese: ステキなお話‥‥。
English: What a lovely story.

Japanese: （ロンドンはイギリスだろう！）
English: (London's in England, not France!)

Japanese: フランスはイイですぞお！ 見せてあげたいですなあ！
English: Oh, yes! France is wonderful. I'd love to show you around the city some time!

Japanese: もう、タマランですぞ！ その‥‥フランスは。
English: It-It's too much! I can't take it! I want... France!

Japanese: ふふふ‥‥
English: Ha ha ha!

Japanese: （千尋さん‥‥失笑してるな）
English: (I can't believe Mia's laughing at the guy... *sigh*)

Japanese: いつも来てくださっている そうですわね。吐麗美庵。
English: You visit Trés Bien a lot, don't you?

Japanese: も、もちろん！ まあ‥‥なんですワイ。
English: Of course! I mean, yes.

Japanese: おじょうさんに会いに行っとる ようなもんですわ！　うははは。
English: I'd like to come and see you there. Heh heh heh.

Japanese: まあ、うれしい。
English: Really? Oh, you're so flattering!

Japanese: 店長ともども、 お待ちしておりますわ。
English: The owner would be delighted to welcome you, I'm sure.

Japanese: あ！　おじょうさん！ あの板前には、気をつけなさいよ。
English: Be careful of that chef, my dear!

Japanese: 板前‥‥って、 シェフの本土坊さんですか？
English: The chef? You mean Mr. Armstrong?

Japanese: おうよ！　なんと言っても、 前科があるからな！
English: That's right. The man's an ex-con.

Japanese: 彼女‥‥いや、彼に “前科”ですって‥‥！
English: He... He's an ex-con!?

Japanese: 店長さん‥‥いったい、なにを なさったのかしら？
English: Whatever did Mr. Armstrong do!?

Japanese: イイ！　そのシンケンなまなざしが もう‥‥タマランですなコリャ！
English: Oh, no! Those eyes! I can't take this!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Mia's really got this guy eating out of her hand...)

Japanese: あのオトコ‥‥客の持ちものに 手を出すクセがあったんですわ！
English: He steals things. From his customers.

Japanese: お客さんの‥‥？
English: From his customers?

Japanese: 手袋だのハンケチだの、 セコいものが多いらしいけどな。
English: Gloves, handkerchiefs... Little things, mainly.

Japanese: かっぱらい、というヤツですな！ おじょうさんも気をつけないと！
English: He's a pilferer! So you be careful around him, my dear.

Japanese: ‥‥それ‥‥たしかな 情報なんですの？
English: Are you sure about this?

Japanese: モチロンですぞ！ 客にバレてタイホされるところを、
English: Of course! He was arrested for it once.

Japanese: オジさん、目撃しましたからな！ ‥‥コーシー飲みながら。
English: I was there when it happened, having my javaccino.

Japanese: （ホントに常連らしいな‥‥）
English: (He really is a regular!)

Japanese: そうだ、おじょうさん！ ワシが一句、書いてあげよう！
English: Let me write you a little haiku about it.

Japanese: 一句‥‥？
English: A haiku?

Japanese: あのオトコに気をつけるよう‥‥ “警句”というヤツですかな！
English: A Japanese poem. It'll explain all you need to know about that chef.

Japanese: 証拠品《五十嵐のメモ》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Victor's Note added to the Court Record.

Japanese: いいですな！　何かとられたら、 オジさんに相談しなさい！
English: If he takes anything again, you let me know!

Japanese: ヤスいものなら、 オジさんが買ってあげますぞ！
English: If it's not too expensive, I'll buy you a replacement!

Japanese: （コマったおジイさんだな‥‥）
English: (Poor guy. He couldn't do enough for Mia.)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、なるほどくん。
English: OK, Phoenix.

Japanese: 私が協力できるのは、 ここまでね。
English: That's about as much as I can do to help.

Japanese: あ、ありがとうございます。
English: Thanks, Mia.

Japanese: おかげで、重要な情報が 手に入りました！
English: We got some really important information thanks to you.

Japanese: まったく、真宵ったら。 こんなコトで呼び出すんだから‥‥
English: Honestly! I can't believe Maya called me for something like this!

Japanese: あの店の常連なんですよね？ ‥‥オジさん。
English: So, you are a regular at that restaurant, right, sir?

Japanese: モンクだらけなのに、どうして 行くんですか？　吐麗美庵。
English: You're always complaining about the place, so why do you go there?

Japanese: ‥‥オジさん？
English: Sir?

Japanese: 食らえッ！　ハトども！ 食らわんと言うかッ！
English: There you are, you filthy pigeons! You want food? Ha-ha! Take this!

Japanese: （‥‥なんかアヤしいけど‥‥）
English: (Something's not right about this guy...)

Japanese: （かくしゴトがあるなら、 サイコ・ロックが見えるはず）
English: (But if he's hiding something, I should be able to see a Psyche-Lock.)
Oh, wait...

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥今、 勾玉がないんだっけ‥‥）
English: (I don't exactly have the Magatama right now, huh...)

Japanese: （‥‥あれを見つけるほうが 先、みたいだな‥‥）
English: (I need to find it before I can get anywhere with this old man.)
